apps/clevertech/

"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation

Run specific test:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation --test test_g_code_validation.TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit
"""

import frappe
import unittest
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()

        # Create test project
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test machine (M-code)
        cls.machine_code = "M99999999"

        # Create test G-codes
        cls.g_code_1 = "G11111111"
        cls.g_code_2 = "G22222222"

        # Create test item (screw)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Raw Material",
            "stock_uom": "Nos"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create Component Master for test item
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create BOM usage rows for two G-codes
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_1}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_2}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test BOMs
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up test data after all tests"""
        # Delete in reverse order of dependencies
        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        """Set up before each test"""
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Clean up after each test"""
        # Delete any MRs created during test
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request` WHERE custom_project_ = %s", self.project.name)
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request Item`")
        frappe.db.commit()

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code aggregate limit (60), not overall limit (100)"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 70,  # Exceeds G-code 1 limit (60) but within overall (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name  # Links to G-code 1
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("60", str(context.exception))  # Should show G-code limit, not overall

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall CM limit"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 110,  # Exceeds overall limit (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today()
                # No bom_no - manual entry
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("overall limit", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("100", str(context.exception))

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 3: Multiple G-codes have independent limits
    # ============================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code should have its own independent limit"""

        # Create MR for G-code 1 with 60 units (max for G1)
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 60,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()

        # Should be able to create MR for G-code 2 with 40 units (its own limit)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g2.name
            }]
        })
        mr2.insert()  # Should succeed

        # Total = 100 (60 + 40), which equals overall limit
        # But each G-code is within its own limit, so both should pass
        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 4: Cumulative validation across multiple MRs
    # ============================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Multiple MRs for same G-code should be validated cumulatively"""

        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()  # Should succeed (40 < 60)

        # Second MR: 30 units for G-code 1 (total 70 > 60)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 30,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })

        # Should fail: cumulative 70 > 60
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr2.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("Existing MRs", str(context.exception))


# Add more test cases as needed
# - Test PO validation with bom_no
# - Test nested D-code scenarios
# - Test make/buy scenarios
# - Test with fetch_exploded = 1 vs 0

# Clevertech Automated Testing Framework

## Overview

This document describes the automated testing strategy for the Clevertech BOM management system, aligned with your framework design principle:

> **Ensure business-critical functionality, module integrations, and APIs are continuously validated through automated tests — across development, UAT, and production readiness cycles.**

## Test Architecture

### 1. Test Structure

```
apps/clevertech/
└── clevertech/
    └── tests/
        ├── __init__.py
        ├── test_g_code_validation.py         # G-code procurement validation
        ├── test_bom_upload.py                 # BOM upload and validation
        ├── test_component_master.py           # CM calculations and cascades
        ├── test_make_buy_cascade.py           # Make/Buy logic
        └── test_integration_procurement.py    # End-to-end procurement flow
```

### 2. Test Types

#### Unit Tests
- Test individual functions in isolation
- Fast execution (< 1s per test)
- Example: `test_calculate_bom_qty_required()`

#### Integration Tests
- Test module interactions
- Medium execution (1-5s per test)
- Example: `test_mr_validation_with_component_master()`

#### End-to-End Tests
- Test complete business workflows
- Slower execution (5-30s per test)
- Example: `test_bom_upload_to_po_creation_flow()`

## Running Tests

### Local Development

**Run all tests:**
```bash
cd /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench
bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests --app clevertech
```

**Run specific module:**
```bash
bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation
```

**Run specific test case:**
```bash
bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests \
  --app clevertech \
  --module test_g_code_validation \
  --test test_g_code_validation.TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit
```

**Run with verbose output:**
```bash
bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests --app clevertech --verbose
```

### CI/CD Integration

## GitHub Actions Workflow

Create `.github/workflows/tests.yml`:

```yaml
name: Clevertech Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop, feature/*]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]
  schedule:
    # Daily sanity run at 2 AM IST
    - cron: '30 20 * * *'  # 2:30 AM IST = 20:30 UTC

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    services:
      mariadb:
        image: mariadb:10.6
        env:
          MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: root
        ports:
          - 3306:3306
        options: --health-cmd="mysqladmin ping" --health-interval=10s --health-timeout=5s --health-retries=3

      redis:
        image: redis:alpine
        ports:
          - 6379:6379

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v3

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v4
        with:
          python-version: '3.10'

      - name: Install Frappe Bench
        run: |
          pip install frappe-bench
          bench init --skip-redis-config-generation --frappe-branch version-15 frappe-bench
          cd frappe-bench
          bench set-config -g db_host 127.0.0.1
          bench set-config -g redis_cache redis://127.0.0.1:6379
          bench set-config -g redis_queue redis://127.0.0.1:6379

      - name: Install ERPNext
        run: |
          cd frappe-bench
          bench get-app --branch version-15 erpnext

      - name: Install Clevertech
        run: |
          cd frappe-bench
          bench get-app $GITHUB_WORKSPACE

      - name: Create test site
        run: |
          cd frappe-bench
          bench new-site test-site --mariadb-root-password root --admin-password admin
          bench --site test-site install-app erpnext
          bench --site test-site install-app clevertech

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          cd frappe-bench
          bench --site test-site run-tests --app clevertech --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage report
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
        with:
          file: ./frappe-bench/sites/coverage.xml
          fail_ci_if_error: true

      - name: Generate test report
        if: always()
        run: |
          cd frappe-bench
          bench --site test-site run-tests --app clevertech --junit-xml-output test-results.xml

      - name: Publish test results
        uses: EnricoMi/publish-unit-test-result-action@v2
        if: always()
        with:
          files: frappe-bench/test-results.xml
```

## Test Execution Schedule

### When Tests Run

1. **On Every Commit** (CI)
   - Runs automatically via GitHub Actions
   - Blocks merge if tests fail
   - ~10-15 minutes

2. **Before Deployment**
   - Manual trigger: `bench --site <site> run-tests --app clevertech`
   - Run before `git push` to staging/production
   - Validates deployment readiness

3. **Daily Scheduled Runs**
   - Runs at 2:30 AM IST (configured in GitHub Actions)
   - Catches regressions from external dependencies
   - Sends alert if failures detected

4. **Pre-Release Validation**
   - Run full test suite before version release
   - Includes performance benchmarks
   - Generate test report for release notes

## Test Data Management

### Test Fixtures

Create reusable test data in `tests/fixtures/`:

```python

        "status": "Open"
    }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

def create_test_bom_structure():

```

### Cleanup Strategy

```python
@classmethod
def tearDownClass(cls):
    """Clean up test data after all tests"""
    # Delete in reverse order of dependencies
    frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", cls.mr.name, force=True)
    frappe.delete_doc("Component Master", cls.cm.name, force=True)
    frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom.name, force=True)
    frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.item.item_code, force=True)
    frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
    frappe.db.commit()
```

## Test Naming Conventions

```python
# Format: test_<what>_<scenario>_<expected_result>
def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit():
    """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code limit"""
    pass

def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit():
    """When MR has no bom_no, should validate against CM limit"""
    pass

def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits():
    """Each G-code should have its own independent limit"""
    pass
```

## Debugging Failed Tests

### Local Debugging

```bash
# Run with pdb debugger
bench --site test-site run-tests --app clevertech --pdb-on-exception

# Run specific failing test
bench --site test-site run-tests \
  --app clevertech \
  --module test_g_code_validation \
  --test TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit \
  --verbose
```

### Check Test Logs

```bash
# View bench logs
tail -f /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/logs/bench.log

# View site logs
tail -f /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/sites/clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com/logs/web.log
```

## Best Practices

### 1. Test Independence
- Each test should be independent
- Use `setUp()` and `tearDown()` for test data
- Don't rely on test execution order

### 2. Test Data Isolation
- Use unique identifiers for test data (e.g., `TEST-PROJECT-001`)
- Clean up all test data in `tearDown()`
- Don't affect production data

### 3. Assertion Quality
```python
# Bad - vague error message
self.assertTrue(result)

# Good - clear error message
self.assertEqual(
    result["status"],
    "success",
    f"Expected success but got {result['status']}: {result.get('message')}"
)
```

### 4. Test Documentation
```python

    Setup:
    - Component has total_qty_limit = 100
    - G-code 1 has aggregate limit = 60 (from bom_usage)
    - G-code 2 has aggregate limit = 40

    Test:
    - Create MR with bom_no pointing to G-code 1
    - Try to add 70 units (exceeds G-code limit but within overall)

    - Should show limit of 60, not 100
    """

```

content.text:
Great question! Let me explain this simply.

## How Automated Testing Works (Simple Explanation)

Think of automated tests like a **robot QA person** that checks your system every time you make changes.

### The Basic Idea

1. **Setup** — The test creates fake/temporary data (a fake project, fake items, fake BOMs, fake Component Masters) inside your actual Frappe database
2. **Action** — It tries to do something (e.g., create a Material Request with qty=70)
3. **Check** — It verifies the result ("Did the system throw an error? Did the error mention the G-code limit?")
4. **Cleanup** — It deletes all the fake data it created, leaving your database clean

### Example in Plain English

```
Test: "MR should fail if qty exceeds G-code limit"

1. Create a fake Component Master with G-code limit = 60
2. Try creating an MR requesting 70 units
3. Check: Did frappe throw a ValidationError? YES → Test PASSES
4. Delete the fake Component Master and MR
```

## Do You Need Real BOMs and Components?

**No — the tests create their own temporary test data.** That's the whole point.

Look at the `setUpClass` in the test file — it creates:
- A fake project (`TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION`)
- A fake item (`TEST-SCREW-M5`)
- A fake Component Master with `total_qty_limit = 100`
- Fake BOM usage rows (G-code 1 = 60 qty, G-code 2 = 40 qty)
- Fake BOMs

All of this gets **deleted automatically** in `tearDownClass` after tests finish.

### BUT — there's a catch

The test data needs to be **realistic enough** to trigger your validation code paths. For example:
- The fake Component Master needs `bom_usage` rows with `g_code` fields (because that's what your validation queries)
- The fake MR needs `custom_project_` and `custom_machine_code` fields set (because your validation filters on those)

If the test data structure doesn't match what the real validation code expects, the tests will either pass falsely or fail for the wrong reasons.

## What Actually Happens When You Run Tests

```
bench --site your-site run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation
```

This does:
1. Frappe starts a **test transaction** (like a database sandbox)
2. Runs each `test_*` method one by one
3. Each test creates data → does something → checks result → cleans up
4. Prints results:
   ```
   test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit ... OK
   test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit ... OK
   test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits ... OK
   test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs ... FAIL
   ```
5. `OK` = your code works correctly, `FAIL` = something is broken

## The Real Value

**Without tests:** You change validation code → manually open browser → create MR → check if error appears → repeat for 10 edge cases → takes 30 minutes → you forget to test some cases

**With tests:** You change validation code → run one command → all 10 edge cases checked in 30 seconds → nothing forgotten

## What Edge Cases Need Real-World Thinking

The test file from the previous conversation covers 4 basic cases. For thorough testing, you'd want to also test:
- Items that exist in **multiple machines** (the machine_code isolation issue)
- Nested D→D→D hierarchies where g_code propagates up
- `fetch_exploded = 1` vs `0` scenarios
- Zero qty limit (`total_qty_limit = 0` means "zero allowed", not "unlimited")
- Cancelled MRs (should not count toward cumulative qty)

These don't need real production data — but they need someone who **understands the business logic** (you) to define what "correct" means for each scenario. The test just encodes that knowledge into code.

"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation

Run specific test:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation --test test_g_code_validation.TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit
"""

import frappe
import unittest
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()

        # Create test project
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test machine (M-code)
        cls.machine_code = "M99999999"

        # Create test G-codes
        cls.g_code_1 = "G11111111"
        cls.g_code_2 = "G22222222"

        # Create test item (screw)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Raw Material",
            "stock_uom": "Nos"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create Component Master for test item
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create BOM usage rows for two G-codes
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_1}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_2}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test BOMs
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up test data after all tests"""
        # Delete in reverse order of dependencies
        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        """Set up before each test"""
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Clean up after each test"""
        # Delete any MRs created during test
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request` WHERE custom_project_ = %s", self.project.name)
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request Item`")
        frappe.db.commit()

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code aggregate limit (60), not overall limit (100)"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 70,  # Exceeds G-code 1 limit (60) but within overall (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name  # Links to G-code 1
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("60", str(context.exception))  # Should show G-code limit, not overall

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall CM limit"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 110,  # Exceeds overall limit (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today()
                # No bom_no - manual entry
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("overall limit", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("100", str(context.exception))

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 3: Multiple G-codes have independent limits
    # ============================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code should have its own independent limit"""

        # Create MR for G-code 1 with 60 units (max for G1)
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 60,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()

        # Should be able to create MR for G-code 2 with 40 units (its own limit)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g2.name
            }]
        })
        mr2.insert()  # Should succeed

        # Total = 100 (60 + 40), which equals overall limit
        # But each G-code is within its own limit, so both should pass
        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 4: Cumulative validation across multiple MRs
    # ============================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Multiple MRs for same G-code should be validated cumulatively"""

        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()  # Should succeed (40 < 60)

        # Second MR: 30 units for G-code 1 (total 70 > 60)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 30,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })

        # Should fail: cumulative 70 > 60
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr2.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("Existing MRs", str(context.exception))


# Add more test cases as needed
# - Test PO validation with bom_no
# - Test nested D-code scenarios
# - Test make/buy scenarios
# - Test with fetch_exploded = 1 vs 0

content.text:
Here's what the 4 current test cases cover, in plain language:

## Current Test Coverage

### Test 1: G-code limit kicks in when BOM is specified
**Scenario:** MR has `bom_no` pointing to G-code 1 (limit = 60), requesting 70 units

| What it checks | Detail |
|---|---|
| MR qty = 70, G-code limit = 60 | Should **FAIL** |
| Error mentions "G-code G11111111" | Confirms G-code-level validation triggered |
| Error mentions "60" (not "100") | Confirms it used the G-code limit, not the overall CM limit |

**Business meaning:** When procurement comes from a specific BOM path, the tighter G-code limit applies.

---

### Test 2: Overall CM limit applies when no BOM specified
**Scenario:** MR has **no** `bom_no` (manual entry), requesting 110 units

| What it checks | Detail |
|---|---|
| MR qty = 110, overall limit = 100 | Should **FAIL** |
| Error mentions "overall limit" | Confirms fallback to CM-level validation |
| Error mentions "100" | Confirms it used the overall limit |

**Business meaning:** When someone manually creates an MR without a BOM reference, the system still validates against the CM's total_qty_limit.

---

### Test 3: G-codes are independent of each other
**Scenario:** Create two MRs — one for G-code 1 (60 units), one for G-code 2 (40 units)

| What it checks | Detail |
|---|---|
| G-code 1: 60 units (limit 60) | Should **PASS** |
| G-code 2: 40 units (limit 40) | Should **PASS** |
| Total = 100 (equals overall limit) | Both succeed because each G-code is within its own limit |

**Business meaning:** Ordering 60 screws for Assembly-1 doesn't eat into Assembly-2's quota of 40 screws. Each G-code tracks independently.

---

### Test 4: Cumulative qty across multiple MRs
**Scenario:** Two separate MRs for the same G-code 1 — first 40 units, then 30 units (total 70)

| What it checks | Detail |
|---|---|
| First MR: 40 units (< 60 limit) | Should **PASS** |
| Second MR: 30 units (cumulative 70 > 60) | Should **FAIL** |
| Error mentions "Existing MRs" | Confirms it counted the first MR's qty |

**Business meaning:** The system remembers previous MRs. You can't bypass the limit by splitting into multiple smaller requests.

---

## What's NOT Covered Yet

The test file itself notes these gaps at the bottom (line 253-257):

| Missing Test | Why It Matters |
|---|---|
| **PO validation** | Only MR is tested — same logic exists in `purchase_order_validation.py` but untested |
| **Nested D-code scenarios** | D→D→D hierarchy where g_code propagates up — does the right G-code get picked? |
| **Make/Buy scenarios** | What happens if item is "Make" not "Buy"? Should validation skip it? |
| **fetch_exploded = 1 vs 0** | BOM explosion changes which items appear in MR — does validation still work? |
| **Cancelled MR not counted** | If you cancel an MR, does cumulative qty go down? (Currently queries `docstatus != 2`) |
| **Zero qty limit (0 vs NULL)** | `total_qty_limit = 0` means "zero allowed", `NULL` means "no limit" — is that tested? |
| **Cross-machine isolation** | Same item in two machines — does it validate against the right CM? |

In short: the 4 tests cover the **happy path of G-code validation for Material Requests only**. The critical edge cases and PO validation are still TODO.

content.thinking: The user wants to understand how test data is created and whether it's temporary. Let me explain based on what I read in the test file.

content.text:
Yes, all temporary. Here's how it works in this test file:

## Data Lifecycle

### 1. Created ONCE before all tests — `setUpClass` (line 23-88)

```
setUpClass runs
    ├── Creates Project ("TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION")
    ├── Creates Item ("TEST-SCREW-M5")
    ├── Creates Component Master (limit=100, with 2 bom_usage rows)
    ├── Creates BOM for G-code 1
    ├── Creates BOM for G-code 2
    └── frappe.db.commit()  ← actually saved to database
```

This data is **shared across all 4 tests** — it's the "stage" that every test performs on.

### 2. Each test creates its OWN MRs — `test_*` methods

Each test creates Material Requests as needed (the thing being tested). For example, Test 4 creates two MRs.

### 3. MRs cleaned up AFTER EACH test — `tearDown` (line 106-111)

```python
frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request` WHERE custom_project_ = %s", self.project.name)
frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request Item`")
```

This runs after **every single test**, so Test 3's MRs don't affect Test 4's results.

### 4. Everything deleted AFTER all tests — `tearDownClass` (line 91-100)

```
tearDownClass runs
    ├── Deletes Component Master
    ├── Deletes BOM 1
    ├── Deletes BOM 2
    ├── Deletes Item
    ├── Deletes Project
    └── frappe.db.commit()  ← cleanup committed
```

## Visual Timeline

```
START
  │
  ├─ setUpClass ──────── Create Project, Item, CM, BOMs (persists)
  │
  ├─ Test 1 ──────────── Creates MR → checks error → tearDown deletes MR
  ├─ Test 2 ──────────── Creates MR → checks error → tearDown deletes MR
  ├─ Test 3 ──────────── Creates 2 MRs → checks OK → tearDown deletes MRs
  ├─ Test 4 ──────────── Creates 2 MRs → checks error → tearDown deletes MRs
  │
  ├─ tearDownClass ───── Delete Project, Item, CM, BOMs
  │
END (database is clean)
```

## Important detail

The data **does go into your real database** during the test run — it's not some in-memory sandbox. That's why the test uses obvious fake names like `TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION` and `TEST-SCREW-M5`, so there's no collision with real data. And `tearDownClass` ensures it's all cleaned up afterward.

If the test crashes mid-way (e.g., you hit Ctrl+C), the test data could be left behind in your database. You'd need to manually delete the `TEST-*` records. That's the one risk with this approach.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation

Run specific test:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation --test test_g_code_validation.TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit
"""

import frappe
import unittest
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()

        # Create test project
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test machine (M-code)
        cls.machine_code = "M99999999"

        # Create test G-codes
        cls.g_code_1 = "G11111111"
        cls.g_code_2 = "G22222222"

        # Create test item (screw)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Raw Material",
            "stock_uom": "Nos"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create Component Master for test item
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create BOM usage rows for two G-codes
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_1}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_2}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test BOMs
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up test data after all tests"""
        # Delete in reverse order of dependencies
        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        """Set up before each test"""
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Clean up after each test"""
        # Delete any MRs created during test
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request` WHERE custom_project_ = %s", self.project.name)
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request Item`")
        frappe.db.commit()

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code aggregate limit (60), not overall limit (100)"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 70,  # Exceeds G-code 1 limit (60) but within overall (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name  # Links to G-code 1
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("60", str(context.exception))  # Should show G-code limit, not overall

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall CM limit"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 110,  # Exceeds overall limit (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today()
                # No bom_no - manual entry
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("overall limit", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("100", str(context.exception))

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 3: Multiple G-codes have independent limits
    # ============================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code should have its own independent limit"""

        # Create MR for G-code 1 with 60 units (max for G1)
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 60,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()

        # Should be able to create MR for G-code 2 with 40 units (its own limit)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g2.name
            }]
        })
        mr2.insert()  # Should succeed

        # Total = 100 (60 + 40), which equals overall limit
        # But each G-code is within its own limit, so both should pass
        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 4: Cumulative validation across multiple MRs
    # ============================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Multiple MRs for same G-code should be validated cumulatively"""

        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()  # Should succeed (40 < 60)

        # Second MR: 30 units for G-code 1 (total 70 > 60)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 30,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })

        # Should fail: cumulative 70 > 60
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr2.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("Existing MRs", str(context.exception))


# Add more test cases as needed
# - Test PO validation with bom_no
# - Test nested D-code scenarios
# - Test make/buy scenarios
# - Test with fetch_exploded = 1 vs 0

"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation

Run specific test:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation --test test_g_code_validation.TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit
"""

import frappe
import unittest
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()

        # Create test project
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test machine (M-code)
        cls.machine_code = "M99999999"

        # Create test G-codes
        cls.g_code_1 = "G11111111"
        cls.g_code_2 = "G22222222"

        # Create test item (screw)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Raw Material",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "73181500"  # HSN code for screws (required by India Compliance)
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create Component Master for test item
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create BOM usage rows for two G-codes
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_1}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_2}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test BOMs
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up test data after all tests"""
        # Delete in reverse order of dependencies
        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        """Set up before each test"""
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Clean up after each test"""
        # Delete any MRs created during test
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request` WHERE custom_project_ = %s", self.project.name)
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request Item`")
        frappe.db.commit()

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code aggregate limit (60), not overall limit (100)"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 70,  # Exceeds G-code 1 limit (60) but within overall (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name  # Links to G-code 1
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("60", str(context.exception))  # Should show G-code limit, not overall

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall CM limit"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 110,  # Exceeds overall limit (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today()
                # No bom_no - manual entry
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("overall limit", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("100", str(context.exception))

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 3: Multiple G-codes have independent limits
    # ============================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code should have its own independent limit"""

        # Create MR for G-code 1 with 60 units (max for G1)
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 60,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()

        # Should be able to create MR for G-code 2 with 40 units (its own limit)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g2.name
            }]
        })
        mr2.insert()  # Should succeed

        # Total = 100 (60 + 40), which equals overall limit
        # But each G-code is within its own limit, so both should pass
        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 4: Cumulative validation across multiple MRs
    # ============================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Multiple MRs for same G-code should be validated cumulatively"""

        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()  # Should succeed (40 < 60)

        # Second MR: 30 units for G-code 1 (total 70 > 60)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 30,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })

        # Should fail: cumulative 70 > 60
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr2.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("Existing MRs", str(context.exception))

""" Material Request Quantity Validation for Project Component Master Validates MR quantities against Component Master total_qty_limit. Hard block (frappe.throw) if quantity exceeds limit. Warning for loose item over-procurement. """ import frappe from frappe import _ def validate_material_request_qty(doc, method=None): """ Validate MR quantities against Project Component Master limits. Hook: Material Request validate event. Only enforces limits for "Buy" items (items that are actually procured). "Make" items are assembled in-house and don't need MR validation. Skips silently if no project is set on the MR. """ # Material Request uses custom_project_ field (with trailing underscore) project = doc.get("custom_project_") if not project: return # No project, skip validation # Bug Fix 2026-02-09: Derive machine_code from cost_center for CM filtering machine_code = doc.get("custom_machine_code") if not machine_code and doc.get("custom_cost_center"): # Fallback: derive from Cost Center (for older MRs before custom_machine_code existed) machine_code = frappe.db.get_value( "Cost Center", doc.custom_cost_center, "custom_machine_code" ) for item in doc.items: _validate_item_qty(project, item.item_code, item.qty, doc.name, machine_code, item.get("bom_no")) def _validate_item_qty(project, item_code, mr_qty, mr_name, machine_code=None, bom_no=None): """ Validate individual item quantity against Component Master limit. Only enforces for "Buy" items — "Make" items are not directly procured. Decision 20 (2026-02-10): G-code level validation - If MR item has bom_no (from "Get Items from BOM"), validate against G-code aggregate limit - Otherwise, validate against CM-level total_qty_limit Args: project: Project name item_code: Item code mr_qty: Requested quantity in this MR mr_name: MR document name (excluded from existing qty calculation) machine_code: Machine code for filtering (Bug Fix 2026-02-09) bom_no: BOM reference (from "Get Items from BOM" button) (Decision 20) """ # Bug Fix 2026-02-09: Add machine_code filter to prevent cross-machine CM collision # When item exists in multiple machines, pick the correct CM for this machine filters = {"project": project, "item_code": item_code} if machine_code: filters["machine_code"] = machine_code component = frappe.db.get_value( "Project Component Master", filters, [ "name", "total_qty_limit", "total_qty_procured", "is_loose_item", "loose_qty_required", "bom_qty_required", "bom_conversion_status", "make_or_buy", ], as_dict=True, ) if not component: return # Item not in Component Master, no restriction (old projects) # Block "Make" items — they are assembled in-house, not procured if component.make_or_buy == "Make": frappe.throw( _( "Cannot add {0} to Material Request.

" "Item is marked as 'Make' in Project Component Master

" "'Make' items are assembled in-house from their child components. " "Only the child parts (marked as 'Buy') should be procured.

" " " "If this item should be purchased, update Make/Buy flag in " "{1}" ).format( frappe.bold(item_code), component.name, ), title=_("Cannot Procure 'Make' Item"), ) # Bug Fix 2026-02-09: Removed total_qty_limit early return check # Both 0 and NULL should block procurement (0 = parent is Buy, NULL = not calculated) # Only items without Component Master (old projects) skip validation # Decision 20 (2026-02-10): G-code level validation # If MR has bom_no (from "Get Items from BOM"), validate against G-code-specific limit qty_limit = component.total_qty_limit or 0 validation_context = "overall limit" if bom_no: # Extract G-code from BOM's item field g_code_item = frappe.db.get_value("BOM", bom_no, "item") if g_code_item: # Load Component Master with bom_usage child table cm_doc = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", component.name) # Sum total_qty_required for all bom_usage rows matching this G-code g_code_limit = sum( row.total_qty_required or 0 for row in cm_doc.bom_usage if row.g_code == g_code_item ) if g_code_limit > 0: qty_limit = g_code_limit validation_context = f"G-code {g_code_item}" # Get existing MR quantities (exclude current MR if updating) # Bug Fix 2026-02-09: Filter by machine_code to prevent cross-machine contamination # Note: Material Request uses custom_project_ field (with trailing underscore) if machine_code: existing_mr_qty = frappe.db.sql( """ SELECT COALESCE(SUM(mri.qty), 0) FROM `tabMaterial Request Item` mri INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request` mr ON mri.parent = mr.name INNER JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON mr.custom_cost_center = cc.name WHERE mr.custom_project_ = %s AND mri.item_code = %s AND cc.custom_machine_code = %s AND mr.docstatus < 2 AND mr.name != %s """, (project, item_code, machine_code, mr_name or ""), )[0][0] or 0 else: # Fallback: no machine filtering (old MRs without cost_center) existing_mr_qty = frappe.db.sql( """ SELECT COALESCE(SUM(mri.qty), 0) FROM `tabMaterial Request Item` mri INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request` mr ON mri.parent = mr.name WHERE mr.custom_project_ = %s AND mri.item_code = %s AND mr.docstatus < 2 AND mr.name != %s """, (project, item_code, mr_name or ""), )[0][0] or 0 total_procurement = existing_mr_qty + mr_qty max_allowed = max(0, qty_limit - existing_mr_qty) # Hard limit check if total_procurement > qty_limit: frappe.throw( _( "Cannot add {0} units of {1} to Material Request.

" "Procurement Limit Exceeded ({8}):
" "" "" "" "" "" "" "
Total Limit:{2}
Existing MRs:{3}
This MR:{4}
Total:{5}
Max Allowed:{6}
" "
" "Update quantity limit in Component Master " "{7} if needed" ).format( mr_qty, frappe.bold(item_code), qty_limit, existing_mr_qty, mr_qty, total_procurement, max_allowed, component.name, validation_context, ), title=_("Procurement Limit Exceeded"), ) # Warning for loose item over-procurement if ( component.is_loose_item and component.bom_conversion_status == "Converted to BOM" ): loose_qty = component.loose_qty_required or 0 bom_qty = component.bom_qty_required or 0 if loose_qty > bom_qty: frappe.msgprint( _( "
" " Note: " "Item {0} was procured as loose item ({1} units) " "but BOM requires only {2} units. " "Current MR adds {3} units (Total limit: {4})" "
" ).format( frappe.bold(item_code), loose_qty, bom_qty, mr_qty, component.total_qty_limit, ), indicator="orange", title=_("Over-procurement Warning"), )

"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation

Run specific test:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation --test test_g_code_validation.TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit
"""

import frappe
import unittest
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()

        # Create test project
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test machine (M-code)
        cls.machine_code = "M99999999"

        # Create test G-codes
        cls.g_code_1 = "G11111111"
        cls.g_code_2 = "G22222222"

        # Create test item (screw)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Raw Material",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "73181500"  # HSN code for screws (required by India Compliance)
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create Component Master for test item
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create BOM usage rows for two G-codes
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_1}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_2}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test BOMs
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up test data after all tests"""
        # Delete in reverse order of dependencies
        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        """Set up before each test"""
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Clean up after each test"""
        # Delete any MRs created during test
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request` WHERE custom_project_ = %s", self.project.name)
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request Item`")
        frappe.db.commit()

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code aggregate limit (60), not overall limit (100)"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 70,  # Exceeds G-code 1 limit (60) but within overall (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name  # Links to G-code 1
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("60", str(context.exception))  # Should show G-code limit, not overall

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall CM limit"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 110,  # Exceeds overall limit (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today()
                # No bom_no - manual entry
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("overall limit", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("100", str(context.exception))

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 3: Multiple G-codes have independent limits
    # ============================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code should have its own independent limit"""

        # Create MR for G-code 1 with 60 units (max for G1)
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 60,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()

        # Should be able to create MR for G-code 2 with 40 units (its own limit)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g2.name
            }]
        })
        mr2.insert()  # Should succeed

        # Total = 100 (60 + 40), which equals overall limit
        # But each G-code is within its own limit, so both should pass
        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 4: Cumulative validation across multiple MRs
    # ============================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Multiple MRs for same G-code should be validated cumulatively"""

        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()  # Should succeed (40 < 60)

        # Second MR: 30 units for G-code 1 (total 70 > 60)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 30,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })

        # Should fail: cumulative 70 > 60
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr2.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("Existing MRs", str(context.exception))


# Add more test cases as needed
# - Test PO validation with bom_no
# - Test nested D-code scenarios
# - Test make/buy scenarios
# - Test with fetch_exploded = 1 vs 0

content.text:
Great questions. Let me answer them clearly.

## Does "Get Items from BOM" run in tests?

**No.** That's a client-side button (JavaScript). When you click it in the browser:

1. JS dialog opens → you pick a BOM and warehouse
2. JS calls a Python API method behind the scenes
3. Python returns the exploded items
4. JS populates the MR Items child table with `item_code`, `qty`, `bom_no`, `warehouse`, etc.

In a test, you're creating the MR directly via Python — **no browser, no JS, no button click**. So none of that auto-population happens.

## Do Fetch From fields auto-populate in tests?

**Yes, partially.** Frappe's `fetch_from` fields (e.g., item_name fetched from item_code) DO auto-populate during `insert()` because Frappe runs `set_fetch_from()` in the save cycle.

**But** "Get Items from BOM" is NOT a fetch_from — it's a custom button action. So those fields won't be set automatically.

## The important question for our test

You said the BOM reference is stored in `custom_procurement_bom` on the Material Request header, not `bom_no` on the item row. But the current validation code reads:

```python
# material_request_validation.py, line 37
item.get("bom_no")
```

So the validation is looking at `bom_no` on the **item row**, not `custom_procurement_bom` on the **header**.

Which one should it actually use? Because this affects both the test design and whether the validation code itself is correct. Should the validation read `custom_procurement_bom` from the MR header instead?
it should get from bom_no on the item row

content.text:
You're right, we should set `custom_machine_code`. Here's why I hesitated and why it matters:

## The Cost Center problem

When `custom_machine_code` is set on the MR, the validation's **cumulative qty query** (line 127-140) does this:

```sql
SELECT SUM(mri.qty)
FROM `tabMaterial Request Item` mri
INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request` mr ON mri.parent = mr.name
INNER JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON mr.custom_cost_center = cc.name  -- ← JOIN
WHERE cc.custom_machine_code = %s   -- ← filters through Cost Center
```

So for Test 4 (cumulative check) to work — where the second MR needs to "see" the first MR's qty — the first MR must have `custom_cost_center` set, and that Cost Center must have `custom_machine_code` matching.

## What we need to create

```
Test Cost Center (custom_machine_code = "MT4000084237")
    └── set on every test MR as custom_cost_center

Test MRs:
    custom_machine_code = "MT4000084237"
    custom_cost_center  = <test cost center name>
```

So the setup becomes:

| Test Data | Value |
|---|---|
| Machine code | `MT4000084237` |
| G-code 1 item | `GT3000012345` |
| G-code 2 item | `GT3000067890` |
| Raw material | `TEST-SCREW-M5` |
| Item Group | `Commercial Mechanical` |
| Cost Center | New one with `custom_machine_code = MT4000084237` |
| BOM names | Auto-generated by ERPNext (e.g., `BOM-GT3000012345-001`) |

This way we're testing the **full real code path** including machine_code isolation through Cost Center.
