"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests --app clevertech --module clevertech.tests.test_g_code_validation
"""

import frappe
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

        # --- Test identifiers (T = test) ---
        cls.machine_code = "MT4000084237"
        cls.g_code_1 = "GT3000012345"
        cls.g_code_2 = "GT3000067890"

        # --- Company (needed for Cost Center) ---
        cls.company = frappe.defaults.get_defaults().company

        # --- Project ---
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Cost Center (with custom_machine_code for MR filtering) ---
        root_cc = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Cost Center",
            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1, "parent_cost_center": ("is", "not set")},
            "name"
        ) or frappe.db.get_value(
            "Cost Center",
            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1},
            "name"
        )
        cls.cost_center = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Cost Center",
            "cost_center_name": "Test MT4000084237",
            "company": cls.company,
            "parent_cost_center": root_cc,
            "is_group": 0,
            "custom_machine_code": cls.machine_code
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Items ---
        # G-code assembly items (BOM parents)
        cls.g_item_1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "item_name": "Test G-Code Assembly 1",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "84839000"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.g_item_2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "item_name": "Test G-Code Assembly 2",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "84839000"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Raw material item (the thing being procured)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "73181500"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- BOMs (G-code assemblies containing the raw material) ---
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 2,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 1,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Component Master (for the raw material) ---
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # BOM usage rows — two G-codes with independent limits
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g1.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g2.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up all test data after tests"""
        # Clean up MRs first (child before parent)
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": cls.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)

        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_1.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_2.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Cost Center", cls.cost_center.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Delete MRs created during this test"""
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": self.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()

    # --- Helper ---

    def _make_mr(self, qty, bom_no=None):
        """Create a test Material Request"""
        item_row = {
            "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
            "qty": qty,
            "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
            "warehouse": "Stores - " + frappe.db.get_default("company_abbr"),
        }
        if bom_no:
            item_row["bom_no"] = bom_no

        return frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "custom_cost_center": self.cost_center.name,
            "items": [item_row]
        })

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code limit (60), not overall (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=70, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        # Should fail: 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("60", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall CM limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall limit (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=110)  # No bom_no

        # Should fail: 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn("overall limit", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("100", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 3: Different G-codes have independent limits
    # =========================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code has its own limit — ordering for G1 doesn't reduce G2's quota"""
        # MR for G-code 1: 60 units (its max)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=60, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # MR for G-code 2: 40 units (its max) — should still pass
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g2.name)
        mr2.insert()

        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 4: Cumulative qty across multiple MRs for same G-code
    # =========================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Second MR for same G-code should count first MR's qty"""
        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1 — passes (40 < 60)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # Second MR: 30 more units for G-code 1 — cumulative 70 > 60
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=30, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr2.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)

            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "84839000"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Raw material item (the thing being procured)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "73181500"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- BOMs (G-code assemblies containing the raw material) ---
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 2,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 1,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Component Master (for the raw material) ---
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # BOM usage rows — two G-codes with independent limits
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g1.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g2.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up all test data after tests"""
        # Clean up MRs first (child before parent)
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": cls.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)

        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_1.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_2.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Cost Center", cls.cost_center.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Delete MRs created during this test"""
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": self.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()

    # --- Helper ---

    def _make_mr(self, qty, bom_no=None):
        """Create a test Material Request"""
        item_row = {
            "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
            "qty": qty,
            "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
            "warehouse": "Stores - " + frappe.db.get_default("company_abbr"),
        }
        if bom_no:
            item_row["bom_no"] = bom_no

        return frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "custom_cost_center": self.cost_center.name,
            "items": [item_row]
        })

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code limit (60), not overall (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=70, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        # Should fail: 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("60", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall CM limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall limit (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=110)  # No bom_no

        # Should fail: 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn("overall limit", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("100", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 3: Different G-codes have independent limits
    # =========================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code has its own limit — ordering for G1 doesn't reduce G2's quota"""
        # MR for G-code 1: 60 units (its max)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=60, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # MR for G-code 2: 40 units (its max) — should still pass
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g2.name)
        mr2.insert()

        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 4: Cumulative qty across multiple MRs for same G-code
    # =========================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Second MR for same G-code should count first MR's qty"""
        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1 — passes (40 < 60)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # Second MR: 30 more units for G-code 1 — cumulative 70 > 60
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=30, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr2.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation

Run specific test:
    bench --site site1.local run-tests --app clevertech --module test_g_code_validation --test test_g_code_validation.TestGCodeValidation.test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit
"""

import frappe
import unittest
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()

        # Create test project
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test machine (M-code)
        cls.machine_code = "M99999999"

        # Create test G-codes
        cls.g_code_1 = "G11111111"
        cls.g_code_2 = "G22222222"

        # Create test item (screw)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Raw Material",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "73181500"  # HSN code for screws (required by India Compliance)
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create Component Master for test item
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create BOM usage rows for two G-codes
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_1}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": f"BOM-{cls.g_code_2}-001",
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Create test BOMs
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up test data after all tests"""
        # Delete in reverse order of dependencies
        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        """Set up before each test"""
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Clean up after each test"""
        # Delete any MRs created during test
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request` WHERE custom_project_ = %s", self.project.name)
        frappe.db.sql("DELETE FROM `tabMaterial Request Item`")
        frappe.db.commit()

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code aggregate limit (60), not overall limit (100)"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 70,  # Exceeds G-code 1 limit (60) but within overall (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name  # Links to G-code 1
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("60", str(context.exception))  # Should show G-code limit, not overall

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall limit
    # ============================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall CM limit"""

        mr = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 110,  # Exceeds overall limit (100)
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today()
                # No bom_no - manual entry
            }]
        })

        # Should throw error because 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr.insert()

        self.assertIn("overall limit", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("100", str(context.exception))

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 3: Multiple G-codes have independent limits
    # ============================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code should have its own independent limit"""

        # Create MR for G-code 1 with 60 units (max for G1)
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 60,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()

        # Should be able to create MR for G-code 2 with 40 units (its own limit)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g2.name
            }]
        })
        mr2.insert()  # Should succeed

        # Total = 100 (60 + 40), which equals overall limit
        # But each G-code is within its own limit, so both should pass
        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # ============================================================================
    # TEST CASE 4: Cumulative validation across multiple MRs
    # ============================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Multiple MRs for same G-code should be validated cumulatively"""

        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1
        mr1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 40,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })
        mr1.insert()  # Should succeed (40 < 60)

        # Second MR: 30 units for G-code 1 (total 70 > 60)
        mr2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 30,
                "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
                "bom_no": self.bom_g1.name
            }]
        })

        # Should fail: cumulative 70 > 60
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as context:
            mr2.insert()

        self.assertIn("G-code G11111111", str(context.exception))
        self.assertIn("Existing MRs", str(context.exception))


# Add more test cases as needed
# - Test PO validation with bom_no
# - Test nested D-code scenarios
# - Test make/buy scenarios
# - Test with fetch_exploded = 1 vs 0

"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests --app clevertech --module clevertech.tests.test_g_code_validation
"""

import frappe
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

        # --- Test identifiers (T = test) ---
        cls.machine_code = "MT4000084237"
        cls.g_code_1 = "GT3000012345"
        cls.g_code_2 = "GT3000067890"

        # --- Company (needed for Cost Center) ---
        cls.company = frappe.defaults.get_defaults().company

        # --- Project ---
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Cost Center (with custom_machine_code for MR filtering) ---
        root_cc = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Cost Center",
            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1, "parent_cost_center": ("is", "not set")},
            "name"
        ) or frappe.db.get_value(
            "Cost Center",
            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1},
            "name"
        )
        cls.cost_center = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Cost Center",
            "cost_center_name": "Test MT4000084237",
            "company": cls.company,
            "parent_cost_center": root_cc,
            "is_group": 0,
            "custom_machine_code": cls.machine_code
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Items ---
        # G-code assembly items (BOM parents)
        cls.g_item_1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "item_name": "Test G-Code Assembly 1",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "84839000"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.g_item_2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "item_name": "Test G-Code Assembly 2",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "84839000"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Raw material item (the thing being procured)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "73181500"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- BOMs (G-code assemblies containing the raw material) ---
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 2,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 1,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Component Master (for the raw material) ---
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # BOM usage rows — two G-codes with independent limits
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g1.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g2.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up all test data after tests"""
        # Clean up MRs first (child before parent)
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": cls.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)

        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_1.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_2.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Cost Center", cls.cost_center.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Delete MRs created during this test"""
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": self.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()

    # --- Helper ---

    def _make_mr(self, qty, bom_no=None):
        """Create a test Material Request"""
        item_row = {
            "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
            "qty": qty,
            "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
            "warehouse": "Stores - " + frappe.db.get_default("company_abbr"),
        }
        if bom_no:
            item_row["bom_no"] = bom_no

        return frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "custom_cost_center": self.cost_center.name,
            "items": [item_row]
        })

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code limit (60), not overall (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=70, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        # Should fail: 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("60", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall CM limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall limit (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=110)  # No bom_no

        # Should fail: 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn("overall limit", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("100", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 3: Different G-codes have independent limits
    # =========================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code has its own limit — ordering for G1 doesn't reduce G2's quota"""
        # MR for G-code 1: 60 units (its max)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=60, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # MR for G-code 2: 40 units (its max) — should still pass
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g2.name)
        mr2.insert()

        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 4: Cumulative qty across multiple MRs for same G-code
    # =========================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Second MR for same G-code should count first MR's qty"""
        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1 — passes (40 < 60)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # Second MR: 30 more units for G-code 1 — cumulative 70 > 60
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=30, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr2.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)

        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # Second MR: 30 more units for G-code 1 — cumulative 70 > 60
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=30, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr2.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""
Test Suite for G-Code Level Procurement Validation (Decision 20)

Tests the G-code aggregate validation logic in material_request_validation.py
and purchase_order_validation.py.

Run tests:
    bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests --app clevertech --module clevertech.tests.test_g_code_validation
"""

import frappe
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase


class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Test G-code level validation in MR and PO"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        """Set up test data once for all tests"""
        super().setUpClass()
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

        # --- Test identifiers (T = test) ---
        cls.machine_code = "MT4000084237"
        cls.g_code_1 = "GT3000012345"
        cls.g_code_2 = "GT3000067890"

        # --- Company and warehouse ---
        cls.company = frappe.defaults.get_defaults().company
        abbr = frappe.db.get_value("Company", cls.company, "abbr")
        cls.warehouse = f"Stores - {abbr}"

        # --- Project ---
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Cost Center (with custom_machine_code for MR filtering) ---
        root_cc = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Cost Center",
            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1, "parent_cost_center": ("is", "not set")},
            "name"
        ) or frappe.db.get_value(
            "Cost Center",
            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1},
            "name"
        )
        cls.cost_center = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Cost Center",
            "cost_center_name": "Test MT4000084237",
            "company": cls.company,
            "parent_cost_center": root_cc,
            "is_group": 0,
            "custom_machine_code": cls.machine_code
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Items ---
        # G-code assembly items (BOM parents)
        cls.g_item_1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "item_name": "Test G-Code Assembly 1",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "84839000"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.g_item_2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "item_name": "Test G-Code Assembly 2",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "84839000"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # Raw material item (the thing being procured)
        cls.test_item = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Item",
            "item_code": "TEST-SCREW-M5",
            "item_name": "Test Screw M5",
            "item_group": "Commercial Mechanical",
            "stock_uom": "Nos",
            "gst_hsn_code": "73181500"
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- BOMs (G-code assemblies containing the raw material) ---
        cls.bom_g1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_1,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 2,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.bom_g2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "BOM",
            "item": cls.g_code_2,
            "quantity": 1.0,
            "items": [{
                "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
                "qty": 1,
                "rate": 10,
                "uom": "Nos",
                "stock_uom": "Nos"
            }]
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Component Master (for the raw material) ---
        cls.component = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project Component Master",
            "project": cls.project.name,
            "item_code": cls.test_item.item_code,
            "machine_code": cls.machine_code,
            "make_or_buy": "Buy",
            "total_qty_limit": 100  # Overall limit: 100
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # BOM usage rows — two G-codes with independent limits
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g1.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_1,
            "qty_per_unit": 2,
            "total_qty_required": 60  # G-code 1 needs 60
        })
        cls.component.append("bom_usage", {
            "parent_bom": cls.bom_g2.name,
            "g_code": cls.g_code_2,
            "qty_per_unit": 1,
            "total_qty_required": 40  # G-code 2 needs 40
        })
        cls.component.save(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up all test data after tests"""
        # Clean up MRs first (child before parent)
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": cls.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)

        frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g1.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("BOM", cls.bom_g2.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.test_item.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_1.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Item", cls.g_item_2.item_code, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Cost Center", cls.cost_center.name, force=True)
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Delete MRs created during this test"""
        test_mrs = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Material Request",
            filters={"custom_project_": self.project.name},
            pluck="name"
        )
        for mr_name in test_mrs:
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr_name, force=True)
        frappe.db.commit()

    # --- Helper ---

    def _make_mr(self, qty, bom_no=None):
        """Create a test Material Request"""
        item_row = {
            "item_code": self.test_item.item_code,
            "qty": qty,
            "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
            "warehouse": "Material Staging - CT",
        }
        if bom_no:
            item_row["bom_no"] = bom_no

        return frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": self.machine_code,
            "custom_cost_center": self.cost_center.name,
            "items": [item_row]
        })

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 1: MR with bom_no validates against G-code limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """When MR has bom_no, should validate against G-code limit (60), not overall (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=70, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        # Should fail: 70 > 60 (G-code 1 limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("60", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 2: MR without bom_no validates against overall CM limit
    # =========================================================================

    def test_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """When MR has no bom_no (manual entry), should validate against overall limit (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(qty=110)  # No bom_no

        # Should fail: 110 > 100 (overall limit)
        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn("overall limit", error_msg)
        self.assertIn("100", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 3: Different G-codes have independent limits
    # =========================================================================

    def test_multiple_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """Each G-code has its own limit — ordering for G1 doesn't reduce G2's quota"""
        # MR for G-code 1: 60 units (its max)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=60, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # MR for G-code 2: 40 units (its max) — should still pass
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g2.name)
        mr2.insert()

        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    # =========================================================================
    # TEST 4: Cumulative qty across multiple MRs for same G-code
    # =========================================================================

    def test_cumulative_validation_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Second MR for same G-code should count first MR's qty"""
        # First MR: 40 units for G-code 1 — passes (40 < 60)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(qty=40, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)
        mr1.insert()

        # Second MR: 30 more units for G-code 1 — cumulative 70 > 60
        mr2 = self._make_mr(qty=30, bom_no=self.bom_g1.name)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr2.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {self.g_code_1}", error_msg)

content.text:
Found it. The CM's `validate` method (line 21) calls `calculate_total_qty_limit()` which is at line 261:

```python
def calculate_total_qty_limit(self):
    project = self.project_qty or 0
    bom = self.bom_qty_required or 0
    self.total_qty_limit = max(project, bom)
```

So **`total_qty_limit` is a calculated field** — it's `max(project_qty, bom_qty_required)`. Our test sets `total_qty_limit = 100` directly, but on save it gets overwritten to `max(0, 0) = 0` because we never set `project_qty` or `bom_qty_required`.

**Fix:** Set `project_qty = 100` on the CM instead of `total_qty_limit` directly. That way the calculation gives `max(100, 0) = 100`.

content.text:
Because the CM has a `validate` hook that **recalculates** `total_qty_limit` every time you save:

```python
# project_component_master.py line 261-272
def calculate_total_qty_limit(self):
    project = self.project_qty or 0
    bom = self.bom_qty_required or 0
    self.total_qty_limit = max(project, bom)
```

So even though our test sets `total_qty_limit = 100`, when `cls.component.insert()` runs, the validate hook fires and overwrites it to `max(0, 0) = 0` because `project_qty` and `bom_qty_required` are both empty.

That's exactly what the error showed — `Total Limit: 0`.

By setting `project_qty = 100` instead, the calculation becomes `max(100, 0) = 100`, and `total_qty_limit` ends up as 100 like we want.

content.thinking:
The user is asking a fundamental question about the test approach. They're realizing that:

1. Component Masters and BOMs are created through `bom_upload_enhanced.py` (which parses an Excel file)
2. The CM's calculated fields (like `total_qty_limit`) depend on the full BOM upload flow
3. Creating test data manually (as we're doing) means we bypass the upload flow and get incomplete/incorrect calculated fields

The user is wondering if we should test starting from the BOM upload phase, but that uses Excel import.

Let me think about this...

There are two approaches:

**Approach A: Unit test the validation in isolation**
- Manually create CMs with the RIGHT field values (like setting `project_qty` so `total_qty_limit` calculates correctly)


- This tests the validation logic itself, not the full flow
- Simpler and faster, but tests a narrower scope

**Approach B: Integration test starting from BOM upload**
- Provide a test Excel file
- Run `bom_upload_enhanced.py` to create BOMs and CMs
- Then test MR validation against that data
- More realistic, but more complex to set up
- Would need a test Excel file committed to the repo

**Approach C: Hybrid**
- Use `frappe.db.set_value()` to directly set the calculated fields after creating the CM
- This bypasses the validate hook recalculation
- Quick fix

The real issue is that test data setup is fragile since we're manually recreating what normally flows from the upload process—fields like `total_qty_limit`, `bom_qty_required`, and the `bom_usage` rows all get populated by that complex flow. For proper isolation testing, I could use `frappe.db.set_value()` to inject calculated fields directly after insert, or if `bom_upload_enhanced.py` exposes functions that work with parsed data rather than just Excel files, I could call those directly. A third option is using an actual test Excel file that runs through the full upload flow.

content.text:
That's a really important observation. Let me break down the two approaches:

## Option A: Test validation logic in isolation (what we're doing now)

Manually create CMs and set the right field values. The problem you just saw — `total_qty_limit` gets recalculated to 0 — is because we're bypassing the upload flow. We can work around this with `frappe.db.set_value()` to directly set calculated fields after insert (skips the validate hook).

**Pros:** Fast, simple, tests just the validation code
**Cons:** Test data is "fake" — doesn't match what the real upload produces

## Option B: Start from BOM upload with a test Excel file

Create a small test Excel file, run it through `bom_upload_enhanced.py`, let it create real BOMs and CMs, then test MR validation against that data.

**Pros:** Tests the full real flow, data is exactly like production
**Cons:** More complex setup, slower, depends on upload code working correctly

## Option C: Hybrid (recommended)

Use `frappe.db.set_value()` after CM creation to set the calculated fields directly. This is how Frappe tests commonly handle computed fields — you set the final state you need without going through the full workflow:

```python
# Create CM normally
cls.component.insert()
# Then directly set the calculated field, bypassing validate
frappe.db.set_value("Project Component Master", cls.component.name, "total_qty_limit", 100)
```

This way we're testing **"does the MR validation work correctly when CM has these values?"** — not testing whether the upload sets those values correctly (that would be a separate test).

becasue even based on existing MR we are stopping BOM version version from upload

input.prompt:
I need to understand the BOM upload flow in this Frappe app. Specifically:

1. What is the entry point / whitelisted function in `bom_upload_enhanced.py` that the client calls?
2. What Excel format does it expect (columns, hierarchy structure)?
3. What does it return?
4. How does it create BOMs and Component Masters?

Read these files:
- /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/project_component_master/bom_upload_enhanced.py (focus on the main whitelisted function, parse logic, and Excel format)
- /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/context/index.md (for overview)

Give me a concise summary of:
- The whitelisted function name and its parameters
- The Excel column structure expected
- The hierarchy (M-code → G-code → D-code → raw materials)
- What gets created (BOMs, CMs, bom_usage rows)
- Any important validations that happen during upload

Keep the response focused — I need enough to create a test Excel file programmatically.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary document based on my reading:

## Summary: BOM Upload Flow in Clevertech Frappe App

Based on my analysis of the code, here's what you need to know to create test Excel files programmatically:

---

### **1. Entry Point (Whitelisted Function)**

**Function:** `create_boms_with_validation(docname)`  
**Location:** `/home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/clevertech/doctype/bom_upload/bom_upload_enhanced.py:194`  
**Parameters:**
- `docname`: Name of a BOM Upload document (must have bom_file, project, and machine_code set)

**Requirements before calling:**
- BOM Upload document must have:
  - `bom_file`: Attached Excel file (binary file)
  - `project`: Project name (required)
  - `machine_code`: Machine code like "P0000000003033" (required for BOM scoping)

---

### **2. Excel Column Structure (Dynamic Mapping)**

The code uses **dynamic column mapping** (Phase 4H) to handle format changes. It reads **row 2** for headers and maps them by name.

**Required headers in row 2:**
| Field Name | Expected Header | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `position` | "Position" | Position/index (optional, can use A/B column fallback) |
| `item_code` | "Item no" | Unique item identifier (M/G/D-code or raw material) |
| `description` | "Description" | Item short description |
| `qty` | "Qty" | Quantity (root assembly only) |
| `revision` | "Rev." | Revision number |
| `extended_description` | "DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA" | Extended description for Item master |
| `material` | "MATERIAL" | Material type |
| `part_number` | "Part_number" | Part number |
| `weight` | "WEIGHT" | Weight value |
| `manufacturer` | "MANUFACTURER" | Manufacturer name |
| `treatment` | "TIPO_TRATTAMENTO" | Surface treatment |
| `uom` | "UM" | Unit of Measure |
| `level` | "LivelloBom" | **CRITICAL:** Hierarchy level (0=root, 1=M, 2=G, 3=D, 4+=raw) |
| `state` (from col J) | "J" column | Design state for G-codes (must be "RELEASED" to be processed) |

**File structure:**
- **Row 1:** Title/header
- **Row 2:** Column headers (exact names above)
- **Row 3+:** Data rows (1 row per component)

---

### **3. Hierarchy Structure**

Excel rows are parsed into a **tree** based on the `level` column:

```
M0000001 (level=0) → ROOT ASSEMBLY
├── G0000001 (level=1) → SUB-ASSEMBLY (design code)
│   ├── D0000001 (level=2) → COMPONENT (design code)
│   │   ├── RAW_MATERIAL_001 (level=3) → RAW MATERIAL
│   │   └── RAW_MATERIAL_002 (level=3) → RAW MATERIAL
│   └── D0000002 (level=2) → COMPONENT
└── G0000002 (level=1) → SUB-ASSEMBLY
    └── RAW_MATERIAL_003 (level=3) → RAW MATERIAL
```

**Hierarchy Rules:**
- **level=0**: Root assembly (M-code by convention)
- **level=1**: Sub-assemblies (typically G-codes = "Design/Geometry")
- **level=2+**: Nested components (D-codes) → leaf items → raw materials
- Each row's level must be > previous row's level OR <= previous row's level (stack-based)
- `build_tree()` uses a stack to match parent-child relationships based on level

---

### **4. What Gets Created**

#### **Step 1: Parse Excel (parse_rows_dynamic)**
- Reads Excel row 3 onwards with dynamic column mapping
- Skips empty rows (no item_code)
- Returns list of parsed rows with structure: `{"item_code", "description", "qty", "level", "children": []}`

#### **Step 2: Build Tree (build_tree)**
- Converts flat list to tree structure using level column
- Parent = any previous row with level < current row's level
- Populates `children` list for each node

#### **Step 3: Create Items (ensure_items_for_all_nodes)**
- Creates Item DocTypes for ALL nodes (assemblies + raw materials)
- Uses existing `ensure_item_exists()` function
- Links images from Excel if available
- Idempotent: skips if Item already exists

#### **Step 4: Create Component Masters (create_component_masters_for_all_items)**
- Creates **Project Component Master** for every item in tree
- Fields set:
  - `project`: from BOM Upload
  - `item_code`: from Excel
  - `machine_code`: from BOM Upload (enables per-machine isolation)
  - `bom_level`: from Excel `level` column
  - `project_qty`: qty from Excel (root assembly only; level 1)
  - `design_status`: from Excel STATE column (default "Design Released")
  - `is_loose_item`: 0 (default)
  - `released_for_procurement`: "No" if item starts with T/Y/E, else "Yes"
  - `make_or_buy`: "Make" if M/G-code, else "Buy" (raw materials)
  - `has_bom`: 1 if assembly (has children), 0 if leaf
  - `active_bom`: NULL (set after BOM creation)
  - `bom_structure_hash`: hash of child structure (assemblies only)
- Updates `make_or_buy` if item already exists + Excel has a value

#### **Step 5: Analyze Upload (analyze_upload)**
- Compares uploaded BOM with existing BOMs via hash
- Categorizes components: `new`, `unchanged`, `changed`, `loose_blocked`
- Checks for blocking: procurement docs on old BOM version, loose item flags
- Returns `can_create` list (components safe to create/update)

#### **Step 6: Create BOMs (create_boms_and_link_components)**
- Uses existing `create_bom_recursive()` for bottom-up BOM creation
- Only creates BOMs for items in `can_create` list
- Recursively processes children

#### **Step 7: Link & Populate (after BOM creation)**
- **Link active_bom**: Points Component Masters → created BOMs
- **Populate hierarchy codes**: Derives `parent_component`, `m_code`, `g_code` from BOM structure
- **Refresh bom_usage rows**: Updates machine_code filter on BOM Usage tables
- **Recalculate**: Syncs `total_qty_limit` and other computed fields

#### **Step 8: BOM Usage population (on_submit hooks)**
- When BOMs are submitted, `bom_hooks.on_submit()` auto-populates BOM Usage rows
- Creates one row per BOM Item with fields: `item_code`, `qty_required`, `parent_cm`, `m_code`, `g_code`, etc.

---

### **5. Return Values**

#### **On Success (Initial Analysis):**
```python
{
    "status": "analysis_complete",
    "analysis": {
        "can_create": [nodes that pass validation],
        "changed_components": [components with version changes],
        "loose_blocked": [loose items not enabled for BOM],
        "blocked_by_dependencies": {...},
        "summary": {
            "total": N,
            "new": N,
            "unchanged": N,
            "changed": N,
            "loose_blocked": N,
            "can_create": N,
            "blocked": N
        }
    }
}
```

#### **On Blocking Issues:**
```python
{
    "status": "blocked",
    "reason": "loose_items_not_enabled" | "hard_blocked_procurement" | ...,
    "analysis": {...},
    "message": "User-friendly error message"
}
```

#### **On Final Creation (after confirmation):**
```python
{
    "status": "success",
    "summary": {
        "items_created": N,
        "items_updated": N,
        "items_failed": N,
        "cms_created": N,
        "cms_updated": N,
        "cms_failed": N,
        "boms_created": N,
        "boms_skipped": N,
        "boms_failed": N,
        ...
    },
    "message": "Upload successful"
}
```

---

### **6. Key Validations During Upload**

| Validation | When | Blocks | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| **G-code STATE filtering** | Parse → Create Components | Full subtree | Skips G-codes with STATE ≠ "RELEASED" |
| **Loose item flag** | Analyze | Can't create BOM | Returns `blocked`, reason `loose_items_not_enabled` |
| **Procurement blocking (hard)** | Analyze | Changed BOM | If MR/RFQ exist on old BOM, blocks until deactivated |
| **Procurement blocking (manager)** | Analyze | Changed BOM | If PO exists, needs manager confirmation |
| **Duplicate detection** | BOM creation | Server-side | ERPNext BOM duplicate check gate (server_scripts) |
| **Hash comparison** | Analyze + Link | None | Detects component version changes for tracking |
| **Machine code isolation** | All steps | None | Prevents cross-machine data contamination |

---

### **7. For Creating Test Excel Files Programmatically**

**Minimal structure:**
```
Row 1: Title/Header (any text)
Row 2: Column headers (exact text):
  A=Position, C=Item no, D=Description, E=Qty, G=Rev., J=<state>, 
  U=DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA, AD=MATERIAL, AE=Part_number, AF=WEIGHT, 
  AG=MANUFACTURER, AL=TIPO_TRATTAMENTO, AN=UM, AQ or AR=LivelloBom

Row 3+: Data rows
  M0000001, M0000001 desc, , 1, , RELEASED, , ...., 0 (level)
  G0000001, G0000001 desc, , , , RELEASED, , ...., 1 (level)
  D0000001, D0000001 desc, , , , , , ...., 2 (level)
  RM001, Raw Material, , , , , , ...., 3 (level)
```

**Key points:**
1. Column headers in row 2 MUST match exactly (case-sensitive)
2. `level` column MUST be numeric and monotonically increasing within parent-child chains
3. `state` = "RELEASED" for G-codes (else filtered out)
4. `qty` required only for root assembly (level=0)
5. Leave blank cells for optional fields

---

This should give you everything needed to programmatically generate test Excel files and understand what the system creates!

## How BOM Upload Works

1. User creates a **BOM Upload** document with `project`, `machine_code`, and attaches an Excel file
2. Calls `create_boms_with_validation(docname)` → parses Excel → creates Items, CMs, BOMs, bom_usage rows, calculates all limits
3. Excel has specific headers in row 2, hierarchy is driven by a `LivelloBom` (level) column

## Test Plan

Create a test Excel programmatically using `openpyxl`:

```
MT4000084237  (level 0, root assembly, qty=1)
├── GT3000012345  (level 1, G-code, state=RELEASED)
│   └── TEST-SCREW-M5  (level 2, raw material, qty=60)
└── GT3000067890  (level 1, G-code, state=RELEASED)
    └── TEST-SCREW-M5  (level 2, raw material, qty=40)
```

After upload, the CM for TEST-SCREW-M5 should get:
- bom_usage row for G1: `total_qty_required = 60`
- bom_usage row for G2: `total_qty_required = 40`
- `total_qty_limit = max(0, 100) = 100`

Then test MR validation against this real data — same 4 test cases but with data created by the actual upload flow.

58:def map_excel_columns(ws):
60:    Dynamically map Excel column letters to field names by searching row 2 headers.
61:    Handles both old and new PE2 export formats where columns may shift.
64:    Hardcoded column AR in parse_rows() caused wrong hierarchy (raw materials got BOMs).
67:        ws: Excel worksheet
70:        dict: Mapping of field names to column letters
74:        frappe.ValidationError: If any required column header is not found
76:    # Define required column headers (exact matches from Excel row 2)
77:    # Based on RCA 2026-02-01: All headers consistent except LivelloBom position
78:    header_mapping = {
79:        # Field name: [List of possible header values in row 2]
95:    # Search row 2 for headers
96:    found_columns = {}
97:    missing_columns = []
99:    # Read all headers from row 2 (up to column 100)
100:    headers_in_excel = {}
102:        col_letter = openpyxl.utils.get_column_letter(col_num)
103:        header_val = ws[f"{col_letter}2"].value
104:        if header_val:
105:            headers_in_excel[col_letter] = str(header_val).strip()
107:    # Map each required field to its column
108:    for field_name, possible_headers in header_mapping.items():
110:        for col_letter, header_val in headers_in_excel.items():
111:            if header_val in possible_headers:
112:                found_columns[field_name] = col_letter
117:            missing_columns.append({
119:                "expected_headers": possible_headers
122:    # Validate all required columns are present
123:    if missing_columns:
124:        error_msg = _("Excel file format validation failed. Missing required columns:")
126:        for missing in missing_columns:
128:            error_msg += f"  Expected header: {', '.join(missing['expected_headers'])}\n\n"
130:        error_msg += f"\nFound headers in row 2:\n"
131:        for col, header in sorted(headers_in_excel.items())[:30]:  # Show first 30 columns
132:            error_msg += f"  Column {col}: {header}\n"
134:        frappe.throw(error_msg, title=_("Invalid Excel Format"))
136:    return found_columns
141:    Parse Excel rows using dynamic column mapping (handles format changes).
144:    Uses map_excel_columns() to find column positions by header name instead of
148:        ws: Excel worksheet
153:    # Get dynamic column mapping (validates required columns exist)
154:    col_map = map_excel_columns(ws)
158:    # Start from row 3 (row 1 = title, row 2 = headers, row 3+ = data)
165:        # Read position (column A or B fallback, same as original)
184:            "state": ws[f"J{r}"].value,  # Design state from column J (G-code release status)
207:        frappe.throw(_("Please attach a BOM Excel file first."))
217:    # Step 1: Parse Excel (reuse existing logic)
232:    rows = parse_rows_dynamic(ws)  # Phase 4H: Dynamic column mapping
