  1476→# ==================== Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and Link ====================
  1477→
  1478→def create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis, ws, image_loader, machine_code=None):
  1479→    """
  1480→    Create BOMs for components that passed analysis, then link active_bom
  1481→    back to their Component Masters.
  1482→
  1483→    Args:
  1484→        tree: List of root nodes
  1485→        project: Project name
  1486→        analysis: Analysis results dict
  1487→        ws: Excel worksheet
  1488→        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
  1489→        machine_code: Machine code to scope hierarchy population (required for multi-machine)
  1490→
  1491→    Returns:
  1492→        dict: {"status": "success", "created": X, "skipped": X, "failed": X, "errors": []}
  1493→    """
  1494→    created = 0
  1495→    skipped = 0
  1496→    failed = 0
  1497→    errors = []
  1498→
  1499→    # Only create BOMs for items in the can_create list
  1500→    can_create_codes = {n["item_code"] for n in analysis["can_create"]}
  1501→
  1502→    for node in tree:
  1503→        try:
  1504→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
  1505→            created += c
  1506→            skipped += s
  1507→            failed += f
  1508→            errors.extend(e)
  1509→        except Exception as ex:
  1510→            failed += 1
  1511→            errors.append(f"{node['item_code']}: {str(ex)}")
  1512→            frappe.log_error(
  1513→                title=f"BOM creation failed for {node['item_code']}",
  1514→                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
  1515→            )
  1516→
  1517→    # Link active_bom back to Component Masters
  1518→    _link_boms_to_component_masters(project)
  1519→
  1520→    # Populate hierarchy codes (parent_component, m_code, g_code) for CMs with missing data
  1521→    # This runs after BOMs are linked so we have the full structure
  1522→    # machine_code filter prevents cross-machine contamination when same item exists in multiple machines
  1523→    _populate_hierarchy_codes(project, machine_code=machine_code, only_missing=True)
  1524→
  1525→    # Refresh bom_usage rows' m_code/g_code from now-correct parent CM headers.
  1526→    # Must run AFTER _populate_hierarchy_codes because add_or_update_bom_usage() was
  1527→    # called before CM headers had m_code/g_code set (ordering issue).
  1528→    _refresh_bom_usage_hierarchy_codes(project, machine_code=machine_code)
  1529→
  1530→    # Recalculate all Component Master quantities (synchronous, after commit)
  1531→    # This ensures total_qty_limit and other calculated fields are populated
  1532→    from clevertech.project_component_master.bom_hooks import recalculate_component_masters_for_project
  1533→    recalc_result = recalculate_component_masters_for_project(project)
  1534→
  1535→    # Add recalculation info to errors if any failed
  1536→    if recalc_result.get("errors"):
  1537→        errors.extend(recalc_result["errors"])
  1538→
  1539→    return {
  1540→        "status": "success",
  1541→        "created": created,
  1542→        "skipped": skipped,
  1543→        "failed": failed,
  1544→        "errors": errors,
  1545→    }
  1546→
  1547→
  1548→def _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader):
  1549→    """
  1550→    Create BOM for a single node using existing create_bom_recursive.
  1551→    Only processes nodes whose item_code is in can_create_codes.
  1552→
  1553→    Args:
  1554→        node: Tree node dict
  1555→        project: Project name
  1556→        can_create_codes: Set of item codes allowed to create BOMs
  1557→        ws: Excel worksheet
  1558→        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
  1559→
  1560→    Returns:
  1561→        tuple: (created, skipped, failed, errors)
  1562→    """
  1563→    created = 0
  1564→    skipped = 0
  1565→    failed = 0
  1566→    errors = []
  1567→
  1568→    item_code = node["item_code"]
  1569→    has_children = bool(node.get("children"))
  1570→    children_count = len(node.get("children", []))
  1571→    in_can_create = item_code in can_create_codes
  1572→
  1573→    if not has_children:
  1574→        # Leaf node — no BOM needed
  1575→        return created, skipped, failed, errors
  1576→
  1577→    if not in_can_create:
  1578→        skipped += 1
  1579→        # DON'T return here - still need to recurse into children!
  1580→    else:
  1581→
  1582→        try:
  1583→            created_flag = create_bom_recursive(node, project, ws, image_loader)
  1584→            if created_flag:
  1585→                created += 1
  1586→            else:
  1587→                skipped += 1
  1588→        except Exception as ex:
  1589→            failed += 1
  1590→            errors.append(f"{item_code}: {str(ex)}")
  1591→
  1592→    # ALWAYS recurse into children (even if current node was skipped)
  1593→    # This ensures we process confirmed child items even when parent is unchanged
  1594→    for child in node.get("children", []):
  1595→        if child.get("children"):
  1596→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(child, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
  1597→            created += c
  1598→            skipped += s
  1599→            failed += f
  1600→            errors.extend(e)
  1601→
  1602→    return created, skipped, failed, errors
  1603→
  1604→
  1605→def _link_boms_to_component_masters(project):
  1606→    """
  1607→    After BOMs are created, link active_bom back to Component Masters.
  1608→    Also populate BOM Usage for linked BOMs (since on_bom_submit doesn't
  1609→    trigger for existing BOMs from other projects).
  1610→
  1611→    Fix (2026-02-04): Process ALL CMs with has_bom=1, not just those without
  1612→    active_bom. This handles the case where BOM is shared across projects -
  1613→    the BOM may have a different project set, so on_bom_submit wouldn't
  1614→    update this project's CM.
  1615→
  1616→    Design: BOMs are shared across projects (no duplicates). When a version
  1617→    change is needed, a new BOM is created only for the affected project.
  1618→    """
  1619→    # Find ALL Component Masters that have BOMs (not just those without active_bom)
  1620→    # This ensures we update active_bom even if it was pointing to an old/wrong BOM
  1621→    cms = frappe.get_all(
  1622→        "Project Component Master",
  1623→        filters={
  1624→            "project": project,
  1625→            "has_bom": 1,
  1626→            # Removed "active_bom not set" filter - process ALL CMs with has_bom=1
  1627→        },
  1628→        fields=["name", "item_code", "active_bom"],  # Include current active_bom
  1629→    )
  1630→
  1631→    # Track BOMs that were linked (not created) - need to populate BOM Usage for these
  1632→    linked_boms = []
  1633→
  1634→    for cm_data in cms:
  1635→        # Find the active default BOM for this item (check with project first)
  1636→        bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
  1637→            "BOM",
  1638→            {
  1639→                "item": cm_data.item_code,
  1640→                "project": project,
  1641→                "is_active": 1,
  1642→                "is_default": 1,
  1643→                "docstatus": 1,
  1644→            },
  1645→            "name",
  1646→        )
  1647→
  1648→        if not bom_name:
  1649→            # Also check without project filter (BOM may belong to another project
  1650→            # but can be shared - this is the "copy-on-write" approach)
  1651→            bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
  1652→                "BOM",
  1653→                {
  1654→                    "item": cm_data.item_code,
  1655→                    "is_active": 1,
  1656→                    "is_default": 1,
  1657→                    "docstatus": 1,
  1658→                },
  1659→                "name",
  1660→            )
  1661→
  1662→        if bom_name:
  1663→            # Only update if active_bom is different (avoid unnecessary saves)
  1664→            if cm_data.active_bom == bom_name:
  1665→                continue  # Already pointing to correct BOM, skip
  1666→
  1667→            cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm_data.name)
  1668→            cm.active_bom = bom_name
  1669→
  1670→            # Recalculate hash from actual BOM doc
  1671→            try:
  1672→                bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name)
  1673→                cm.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
  1674→            except Exception:
  1675→                pass
  1676→
  1677→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
  1678→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
  1679→            cm.save(ignore_permissions=True)
  1680→
  1681→            # Track this BOM for BOM Usage population
  1682→            linked_boms.append({"name": bom_name, "item": cm_data.item_code})
  1683→
  1684→    # Populate BOM Usage for linked BOMs (since on_bom_submit didn't trigger)
  1685→    # This handles the case where BOMs already existed but Component Masters were just created
  1686→    if linked_boms:
  1687→        populate_bom_usage_tables(project, linked_boms)
  1688→
  1689→
  1690→def _populate_hierarchy_codes(project, machine_code=None, only_missing=True):
  1691→    """
  1692→    Populate parent_component, m_code, g_code for Component Masters in a project.
  1693→
  1694→    This function is called after BOMs are linked to ensure hierarchy data is complete.
  1695→    It uses the BOM structure to derive:
  1696→    - parent_component: from active_bom's parent item
  1697→    - m_code: item starting with "M" in the hierarchy
  1698→    - g_code: item starting with "G" in the hierarchy
  1699→
  1700→    Args:
  1701→        project: Project name
  1702→        machine_code: Machine code to filter by (prevents cross-machine contamination)
  1703→        only_missing: If True, only update CMs where fields are NULL (default)
  1704→                      If False, force refresh ALL CMs
  1705→
  1706→    Returns:
  1707→        dict: {"updated": count, "skipped": count}
  1708→    """
  1709→    # Build filters based on only_missing flag
  1710→    # Include machine_code filter to prevent cross-machine contamination
  1711→    filters = {"project": project}
  1712→    if machine_code:
  1713→        filters["machine_code"] = machine_code
  1714→
  1715→    if only_missing:
  1716→        # Get CMs where ANY of the hierarchy fields are missing
  1717→        sql_conditions = ["project = %(project)s"]
  1718→        sql_params = {"project": project}
  1719→
  1720→        if machine_code:
  1721→            sql_conditions.append("machine_code = %(machine_code)s")
  1722→            sql_params["machine_code"] = machine_code
  1723→
  1724→        cms = frappe.db.sql(f"""
  1725→            SELECT name, item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code
  1726→            FROM `tabProject Component Master`
  1727→            WHERE {" AND ".join(sql_conditions)}
  1728→            AND (
  1729→                parent_component IS NULL OR parent_component = ''
  1730→                OR m_code IS NULL OR m_code = ''
  1731→                OR g_code IS NULL OR g_code = ''
  1732→            )
  1733→        """, sql_params, as_dict=True)
  1734→    else:
  1735→        cms = frappe.get_all(
  1736→            "Project Component Master",
  1737→            filters=filters,
  1738→            fields=["name", "item_code", "parent_component", "m_code", "g_code"]
  1739→        )
  1740→
  1741→    if not cms:
  1742→        return {"updated": 0, "skipped": 0}
  1743→
  1744→    updated = 0
  1745→    skipped = 0
  1746→
  1747→    # Build a lookup of item_code → CM name for parent lookups
  1748→    # Filter by machine_code to prevent cross-machine contamination
  1749→    all_cms = frappe.get_all(
  1750→        "Project Component Master",
  1751→        filters=filters,
  1752→        fields=["name", "item_code"]
  1753→    )
  1754→    item_to_cm = {cm.item_code: cm.name for cm in all_cms}
  1755→
  1756→    for cm_data in cms:
  1757→        item_code = cm_data.item_code
  1758→        has_changes = False
  1759→
  1760→        # Derive m_code and g_code from item_code itself
  1761→        derived_m_code = None
  1762→        derived_g_code = None
  1763→
  1764→        if item_code.startswith("M"):
  1765→            # This IS an M-code
  1766→            derived_m_code = item_code
  1767→            derived_g_code = None
  1768→        elif item_code.startswith("G"):
  1769→            # This IS a G-code, need to find its M-code from parent
  1770→            derived_g_code = item_code
  1771→            # Find parent via BOM structure
  1772→            parent_item = _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project)
  1773→            if parent_item and parent_item.startswith("M"):
  1774→                derived_m_code = parent_item
  1775→            elif parent_item:
  1776→                # Parent is not M-code, try to get m_code from parent CM
  1777→                parent_cm_name = item_to_cm.get(parent_item)
  1778→                if parent_cm_name:
  1779→                    derived_m_code = frappe.db.get_value(
  1780→                        "Project Component Master", parent_cm_name, "m_code"
  1781→                    )
  1782→        else:
  1783→            # This is below G-level (D-code, raw material, etc.)
  1784→            # Find parent and derive both m_code and g_code
  1785→            parent_item = _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project)
  1786→            if parent_item:
  1787→                parent_cm_name = item_to_cm.get(parent_item)
  1788→                if parent_cm_name:
  1789→                    parent_cm_data = frappe.db.get_value(
  1790→                        "Project Component Master",
  1791→                        parent_cm_name,
  1792→                        ["m_code", "g_code", "item_code"],
  1793→                        as_dict=True
  1794→                    )
  1795→                    if parent_cm_data:
  1796→                        # Inherit m_code from parent
  1797→                        derived_m_code = parent_cm_data.m_code
  1798→
  1799→                        # If parent is G-code, use it as g_code
  1800→                        if parent_cm_data.item_code.startswith("G"):
  1801→                            derived_g_code = parent_cm_data.item_code
  1802→                        else:
  1803→                            # Otherwise inherit parent's g_code
  1804→                            derived_g_code = parent_cm_data.g_code
  1805→
  1806→        # Determine parent_component
  1807→        derived_parent_component = None
  1808→        parent_item = _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project)
  1809→        if parent_item:
  1810→            derived_parent_component = item_to_cm.get(parent_item)
  1811→
  1812→        # Check what needs updating
  1813→        needs_parent = (not cm_data.parent_component) and derived_parent_component
  1814→        needs_m_code = (not cm_data.m_code) and derived_m_code
  1815→        needs_g_code = (not cm_data.g_code) and derived_g_code
  1816→
  1817→        if needs_parent or needs_m_code or needs_g_code or not only_missing:
  1818→            # Update using db_set for efficiency (avoid full doc load/save)
  1819→            updates = {}
  1820→            if needs_parent or (not only_missing and derived_parent_component):
  1821→                updates["parent_component"] = derived_parent_component
  1822→            if needs_m_code or (not only_missing and derived_m_code):
  1823→                updates["m_code"] = derived_m_code
  1824→            if needs_g_code or (not only_missing and derived_g_code):
  1825→                updates["g_code"] = derived_g_code
  1826→
  1827→            if updates:
  1828→                frappe.db.set_value(
  1829→                    "Project Component Master",
  1830→                    cm_data.name,
  1831→                    updates,
  1832→                    update_modified=False
  1833→                )
  1834→                has_changes = True
  1835→
  1836→        if has_changes:
  1837→            updated += 1
  1838→        else:
  1839→            skipped += 1
  1840→
  1841→    if updated > 0:
  1842→        frappe.db.commit()
  1843→
  1844→    return {"updated": updated, "skipped": skipped}
  1845→
  1846→
  1847→def _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project):
  1848→    """
  1849→    Find the parent item of an item by looking at BOM structure.
  1850→
  1851→    Checks which BOMs contain this item as a child and returns the parent item.
  1852→    Prioritizes BOMs with the same project, then any active BOM.
  1853→
  1854→    Args:
  1855→        item_code: Item code to find parent for
  1856→        project: Project name
  1857→
  1858→    Returns:
  1859→        str: Parent item code, or None if not found
  1860→    """
  1861→    # First try: Find BOM with matching project that contains this item
  1862→    parent_item = frappe.db.sql("""
  1863→        SELECT DISTINCT bom.item
  1864→        FROM `tabBOM` bom
  1865→        INNER JOIN `tabBOM Item` bi ON bi.parent = bom.name
  1866→        WHERE bi.item_code = %(item_code)s
  1867→        AND bom.project = %(project)s
  1868→        AND bom.docstatus = 1
  1869→        AND bom.is_active = 1

500→            continue
   501→
   502→        try:
   503→            # Check if item starts with T, Y, or E
   504→            item_code_upper = str(item_code).upper()
   505→            if item_code_upper.startswith(("T", "Y", "E")):
   506→                released_for_procurement = "No"
   507→            else:
   508→                released_for_procurement = "Yes"
   509→
   510→            # Only set project_qty for root assemblies (level 1)
   511→            # Child items (level 2+) get project_qty=0, their requirement comes from bom_qty_required
   512→            level = node.get("level", 0)
   513→            project_qty = node.get("qty", 0) if level == 1 else 0
   514→
   515→            # Map STATE to design_status (from Excel column J)
   516→            # If STATE exists, use it. Otherwise default to "Design Released"
   517→            state = node.get("state")
   518→            if state and str(state).strip():
   519→                design_status = str(state).strip().title()  # Capitalize properly (e.g., "RELEASED" → "Released")
   520→            else:
   521→                design_status = "Design Released"
   522→
   523→            cm_data = {
   524→                "doctype": "Project Component Master",
   525→                "project": project,
   526→                "item_code": item_code,
   527→                "machine_code": machine_code,  # Set machine code on all Component Masters
   528→                "bom_level": level,
   529→                "is_loose_item": 0,
   530→                "design_status": design_status,  # Mapped from Excel STATE column
   531→                "project_qty": project_qty,  # From Excel column E (root assembly only)
   532→                "created_from": "BOM Upload",
   533→                "released_for_procurement": released_for_procurement,
   534→            }
   535→
   536→            # Determine Make/Buy based on item code prefix
   537→            # Items starting with M or G: "Make" (assemblies/sub-assemblies)
   538→            # All other items: "Buy" (raw materials/purchased components)
   539→            item_code_upper = str(item_code).upper()
   540→            if item_code_upper.startswith(("M", "G")):
   541→                default_make_or_buy = "Make"
   542→            else:
   543→                default_make_or_buy = "Buy"
   544→
   545→            if is_assembly:
   546→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 1
   547→                cm_data["active_bom"] = None  # Set later after BOM creation
   548→                cm_data["bom_structure_hash"] = calculate_tree_structure_hash(node["children"])
   549→                # Excel value takes precedence, otherwise use prefix-based default
   550→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or default_make_or_buy
   551→            else:
   552→                # Leaf item (raw material) - use prefix-based default
   553→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 0
   554→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or default_make_or_buy
   555→
   556→            cm = frappe.get_doc(cm_data)
   557→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
   558→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
   559→            cm.insert(ignore_permissions=True)

   561→        except Exception:
   562→            counters["failed"] += 1
   563→            frappe.log_error(

   565→                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
   566→            )
   567→
   568→    return counters

   570→
   571→def _merge_make_or_buy(cm_name, node):
   572→    """
   573→    Merge make/buy from Excel into existing Component Master.
   574→    Only updates if Excel has an explicit value; otherwise keeps existing.
   575→
   576→    This allows PE2 team to only tag new items or changes in the Excel.
   577→    Previously tagged items can be left blank.
   578→
   579→    Returns:
   580→        bool: True if value was updated, False if unchanged
   581→    """
   582→    excel_make_or_buy = node.get("make_or_buy")
   583→    if not excel_make_or_buy:
   584→        return False  # No value in Excel, keep existing
   585→
   586→    current_value = frappe.db.get_value(
   587→        "Project Component Master", cm_name, "make_or_buy"
   588→    )
   589→
   590→    if current_value != excel_make_or_buy:
   591→        frappe.db.set_value(
   592→            "Project Component Master", cm_name,
   593→            "make_or_buy", excel_make_or_buy
   594→        )
   595→        return True
   596→    return False
   597→
   598→
   599→def _get_all_nodes(tree):
   600→    """Flatten tree to get ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)."""
   601→    all_nodes = []
   602→    for node in tree:
   603→        all_nodes.append(node)
   604→        if node.get("children"):
   605→            all_nodes.extend(_get_all_nodes(node["children"]))
   606→    return all_nodes
   607→
   608→
   609→def _get_assembly_nodes(tree):
   610→    """Flatten tree to get all assembly nodes (nodes that have children)."""
   611→    assemblies = []
   612→    for node in tree:
   613→        if node.get("children"):
   614→            assemblies.append(node)
   615→            assemblies.extend(_get_assembly_nodes(node["children"]))
   616→    return assemblies
   617→
   618→
   619→def filter_tree_by_g_code_state(tree):
   620→    """
   621→    Filter tree to remove G-code items with non-released STATE.
   622→
   623→    Business Rule (Approach 1 - G-level only):
   624→    - If item starts with "G" and STATE != "RELEASED" (or blank), skip entire subtree
   625→    - This prevents creation of Items, Component Masters, and BOMs for unreleased designs
   626→
   627→    Args:
   628→        tree: List of root nodes from build_tree()
   629→
   630→    Returns:
   631→        tuple: (filtered_tree, skipped_info)
   632→            filtered_tree: Tree with non-released G-codes removed
   633→            skipped_info: Dict with {"count": N, "items": [...]} for user notification
   634→    """
   635→    filtered_tree = []
   636→    skipped_items = []
   637→
   638→    def should_skip_node(node):
   639→        """Check if this G-code node should be skipped."""
   640→        item_code = str(node.get("item_code", "")).upper()
   641→
   642→        # Only check G-codes
   643→        if not item_code.startswith("G"):
   644→            return False
   645→
   646→        # Get STATE value
   647→        state = node.get("state")
   648→
   649→        # Skip if STATE is blank or not "RELEASED"

content.content:
697→def analyze_upload(tree, project, machine_code):
   698→    """
   699→    Analyze upload tree for new, unchanged, changed, and blocked components.
   700→
   701→    Args:
   702→        tree: List of root nodes
   703→        project: Project name
   704→        machine_code: Machine code from BOM Upload
   705→
   706→    Returns:
   707→        dict with can_create, changed_components, loose_blocked,
   708→        blocked_by_dependencies, summary
   709→    """
   710→    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
   711→    graph = _build_dependency_graph(all_assemblies)
   712→
   713→    categorized = {
   714→        "new": [],
   715→        "unchanged": [],
   716→        "changed": [],
   717→        "loose_blocked": [],
   718→    }
   719→
   720→    for item_code, data in graph.items():
   721→        status, details = _determine_component_status(
   722→            item_code, data["node"], project, machine_code
   723→        )
   724→        data["status"] = status
   725→        data["details"] = details
   726→        categorized[status].append(item_code)
   727→
   728→    # Find blocking dependencies (ancestor chain)
   729→    changed_set = set(categorized["changed"])
   730→    loose_blocked_set = set(categorized["loose_blocked"])
   731→    blocked_by_changes = _find_blocked_ancestors(
   732→        graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set
   733→    )
   734→
   735→    # Build list of items safe to create
   736→    blocked_set = set(blocked_by_changes.keys())
   737→    can_create = []
   738→    for item_code in categorized["new"] + categorized["unchanged"]:
   739→        if item_code not in blocked_set:
   740→            can_create.append(graph[item_code]["node"])
   741→
   742→    return {
   743→        "can_create": can_create,
   744→        "changed_components": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["changed"]],
   745→        "loose_blocked": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["loose_blocked"]],
   746→        "blocked_by_dependencies": blocked_by_changes,
   747→        "summary": {
   748→            "total": len(all_assemblies),
   749→            "new": len(categorized["new"]),
   750→            "unchanged": len(categorized["unchanged"]),
   751→            "changed": len(categorized["changed"]),
   752→            "loose_blocked": len(categorized["loose_blocked"]),
   753→            "can_create": len(can_create),
   754→            "blocked": len(blocked_by_changes),
   755→        },
   756→    }
   757→
   758→
   759→def _determine_component_status(item_code, node, project, machine_code):
   760→    """
   761→    Determine if component is new, unchanged, changed, or loose_blocked.
   762→
   763→    Args:
   764→        item_code: Item code to check
   765→        node: Tree node dict
   766→        project: Project name
   767→        machine_code: Machine code from BOM Upload
   768→
   769→    Returns:
   770→        tuple: (status, details)
   771→    """
   772→    component_master = frappe.db.get_value(
   773→        "Project Component Master",
   774→        {
   775→            "project": project,
   776→            "item_code": item_code,
   777→            "machine_code": machine_code
   778→        },
   779→        ["name", "is_loose_item", "can_be_converted_to_bom",
   780→         "bom_structure_hash", "active_bom"],
   781→        as_dict=True,
   782→    )
   783→
   784→    if not component_master:
   785→        return "new", {"first_time": True}
   786→
   787→    # Check loose item blocking
   788→    if component_master.is_loose_item and not component_master.can_be_converted_to_bom:
   789→        return "loose_blocked", {
   790→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   791→            "message": f"Loose item {item_code} not enabled for BOM conversion",
   792→        }
   793→
   794→    # Check for BOM changes — no active BOM linked to Component Master yet
   795→    if not component_master.active_bom:
   796→        # But check if an active BOM already exists in system (might not be linked yet)
   797→        existing_bom = frappe.db.get_value(
   798→            "BOM",
   799→            {
   800→                "item": item_code,
   801→                "project": project,
   802→                "is_active": 1,
   803→                "is_default": 1,
   804→                "docstatus": 1,
   805→            },
   806→            "name",
   807→        )
   808→
   809→        if not existing_bom:
   810→            # Also check without project filter (BOM may not have project set)
   811→            existing_bom = frappe.db.get_value(
   812→                "BOM",
   813→                {
   814→                    "item": item_code,
   815→                    "is_active": 1,
   816→                    "is_default": 1,
   817→                    "docstatus": 1,
   818→                },
   819→                "name",
   820→            )
   821→
   822→        if not existing_bom:
   823→            # No existing BOM found — truly new
   824→            return "new", {
   825→                "component_master": component_master.name,
   826→                "first_time": False,
   827→            }
   828→
   829→        # BOM exists but not linked to Component Master — compare hashes
   830→        children = node.get("children", [])
   831→        new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   832→
   833→        existing_hash = frappe.db.get_value(
   834→            "BOM", existing_bom, "custom_bom_structure_hash"
   835→        )
   836→
   837→        # Backfill hash if NULL
   838→        if not existing_hash:
   839→            bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", existing_bom)
   840→            existing_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
   841→            if existing_hash:
   842→                frappe.db.set_value(
   843→                    "BOM", existing_bom,
   844→                    "custom_bom_structure_hash", existing_hash,
   845→                    update_modified=False
   846→                )
   847→
   848→        if new_hash == existing_hash:
   849→            # BOM exists and hash matches — unchanged, will be linked later
   850→            return "unchanged", {
   851→                "component_master": component_master.name,
   852→                "bom": existing_bom,
   853→                "existing_bom_not_linked": True,
   854→            }
   855→        else:
   856→            # BOM exists but hash differs — version change needed
   857→            blocking_info = _check_procurement_blocking(project, item_code, existing_bom)
   858→            children = node.get("children", [])
   859→            bom_diff = calculate_bom_diff(existing_bom, children)
   860→            return "changed", {
   861→                "change_type": "bom_structure",
   862→                "component_master": component_master.name,
   863→                "old_bom": existing_bom,
   864→                "old_hash": existing_hash,
   865→                "new_hash": new_hash,
   866→                "blocking_level": blocking_info["blocking_level"],
   867→                "blocking_message": blocking_info["message"],
   868→                "can_proceed": blocking_info["can_proceed"],
   869→                "procurement_docs": blocking_info["procurement_docs"],
   870→                "existing_bom_not_linked": True,
   871→                "bom_diff": bom_diff,
   872→                "impacted_parents": _get_impacted_parent_boms(existing_bom),
   873→            }
   874→
   875→    # Compare BOM structures via hash - get hash from BOM itself (source of truth)
   876→    children = node.get("children", [])
   877→    new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   878→
   879→    # Get existing hash from BOM (not Component Master)
   880→    existing_hash = frappe.db.get_value(
   881→        "BOM", component_master.active_bom, "custom_bom_structure_hash"
   882→    )
   883→
   884→    # Backfill if NULL - calculate from actual BOM items
   885→    if not existing_hash:
   886→        bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", component_master.active_bom)
   887→        existing_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
   888→        if existing_hash:
   889→            # Store the backfilled hash on BOM
   890→            frappe.db.set_value(
   891→                "BOM", component_master.active_bom,
   892→                "custom_bom_structure_hash", existing_hash,
   893→                update_modified=False
   894→            )
   895→
   896→    if new_hash != existing_hash:
   897→        # Check procurement blocking level
   898→        blocking_info = _check_procurement_blocking(
   899→            project, item_code, component_master.active_bom
   900→        )
   901→        bom_diff = calculate_bom_diff(component_master.active_bom, children)
   902→        return "changed", {
   903→            "change_type": "bom_structure",
   904→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   905→            "old_bom": component_master.active_bom,
   906→            "old_hash": existing_hash,
   907→            "new_hash": new_hash,
   908→            "blocking_level": blocking_info["blocking_level"],
   909→            "blocking_message": blocking_info["message"],
   910→            "can_proceed": blocking_info["can_proceed"],
   911→            "procurement_docs": blocking_info["procurement_docs"],
   912→            "bom_diff": bom_diff,
   913→            "impacted_parents": _get_impacted_parent_boms(component_master.active_bom),
   914→        }
   915→
   916→    return "unchanged", {
   917→        "component_master": component_master.name,
   918→        "bom": component_master.active_bom,
   919→    }
   920→
   921→
   922→def _build_dependency_graph(assemblies):
   923→    """
   924→    Build a dependency graph from assembly nodes.
   925→    Maps item_code -> {node, children (item_codes), status, details}
   926→    """
   927→    graph = {}
   928→    for node in assemblies:
   929→        child_codes = [c["item_code"] for c in node.get("children", [])]
   930→        graph[node["item_code"]] = {
   931→            "node": node,
   932→            "children": child_codes,
   933→            "status": None,
   934→            "details": None,
   935→        }
   936→    return graph
   937→
   938→
   939→def _find_blocked_ancestors(graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set):
   940→    """
   941→    Find all components blocked by changed or loose-blocked children.
   942→    Uses iterative approach to propagate blocking up the tree.
   943→
   944→    Returns:
   945→        dict: Mapping of blocked item codes to list of blocking reasons
   946→    """

content.content:
1→"""
     2→Enhanced BOM Upload with Validation and Component Master Integration
     3→
     4→New "Create BOMs with Validation" button on BOM Upload form.
     5→Imports existing functions from bom_upload.py — does NOT modify that file.
     6→
     7→Sequence (Decision 11 & 12):
     8→  1. Parse Excel (reuse parse_rows, build_tree)
     9→  2. Create Items for assemblies (reuse ensure_item_exists)
    10→  3. Create Component Masters FIRST (active_bom=null)
    11→  4. Analyze upload — hash comparison, blocking checks
    12→  5. Create BOMs bottom-up (reuse create_bom_recursive)
    13→  6. Link active_bom back to Component Masters
    14→  7. BOM on_submit hooks auto-populate BOM Usage tables
    15→"""
    16→
    17→import frappe
    18→from frappe import _
    19→import openpyxl
    20→import io
    21→import hashlib
    22→import json
    23→
    24→from clevertech.clevertech.doctype.bom_upload.bom_upload import (
    25→    parse_rows,  # Original - will be replaced by parse_rows_dynamic for enhanced upload
    26→    build_tree,
    27→    ensure_item_exists,
    28→    create_bom_recursive,
    29→    HAS_IMAGE_LOADER,
    30→    # Utility functions (Decision 12A - reuse from original)
    31→    clean_code,
    32→    to_float,
    33→    normalize_uom,
    34→    # Mapping functions (Decision 12A - auto-inherit when migrated to doctypes)

"""
Enhanced BOM Upload with Validation and Component Master Integration

New "Create BOMs with Validation" button on BOM Upload form.
Imports existing functions from bom_upload.py — does NOT modify that file.

Sequence (Decision 11 & 12):
  1. Parse Excel (reuse parse_rows, build_tree)
  2. Create Items for assemblies (reuse ensure_item_exists)
  3. Create Component Masters FIRST (active_bom=null)
  4. Analyze upload — hash comparison, blocking checks
  5. Create BOMs bottom-up (reuse create_bom_recursive)
  6. Link active_bom back to Component Masters
  7. BOM on_submit hooks auto-populate BOM Usage tables
"""

import frappe
from frappe import _
import openpyxl
import io
import hashlib
import json

from clevertech.clevertech.doctype.bom_upload.bom_upload import (
    parse_rows,  # Original - will be replaced by parse_rows_dynamic for enhanced upload
    build_tree,
    ensure_item_exists,
    create_bom_recursive,
    HAS_IMAGE_LOADER,
    # Utility functions (Decision 12A - reuse from original)
    clean_code,
    to_float,
    normalize_uom,
    # Mapping functions (Decision 12A - auto-inherit when migrated to doctypes)
    normalize_material,
    get_surface_treatment,
    get_type_of_material,
    get_item_group_and_hsn,
)

# Conditional import for image loader (same as bom_upload.py)
try:
    from openpyxl_image_loader import SheetImageLoader
except ImportError:
    SheetImageLoader = None

from clevertech.project_component_master.bom_hooks import (
    calculate_bom_structure_hash as calculate_bom_doc_hash,
)

from clevertech.project_component_master.bulk_generation import (
    populate_bom_usage_tables,
)


# ==================== Dynamic Column Mapping (Phase 4H) ====================

def map_excel_columns(ws):
    """
    Dynamically map Excel column letters to field names by searching row 2 headers.
    Handles both old and new PE2 export formats where columns may shift.

    Root Cause (2026-02-01): PE2 export format changed, LivelloBom moved from AR to AQ.
    Hardcoded column AR in parse_rows() caused wrong hierarchy (raw materials got BOMs).

    Args:
        ws: Excel worksheet

    Returns:
        dict: Mapping of field names to column letters
              Example: {"item_code": "C", "level": "AQ", ...}

    Raises:
        frappe.ValidationError: If any required column header is not found
    """
    # Define required column headers (exact matches from Excel row 2)
    # Based on RCA 2026-02-01: All headers consistent except LivelloBom position
    header_mapping = {
        # Field name: [List of possible header values in row 2]
        "position": ["Position"],
        "item_code": ["Item no"],
        "description": ["Description"],
        "qty": ["Qty"],
        "revision": ["Rev."],
        "extended_description": ["DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA"],
        "material": ["MATERIAL"],
        "part_number": ["Part_number"],
        "weight": ["WEIGHT"],
        "manufacturer": ["MANUFACTURER"],
        "treatment": ["TIPO_TRATTAMENTO"],
        "uom": ["UM"],
        "level": ["LivelloBom"],  # CRITICAL: Shifted between formats (AR vs AQ)
    }

    # Search row 2 for headers
    found_columns = {}
    missing_columns = []

    # Read all headers from row 2 (up to column 100)
    headers_in_excel = {}

Args: docname: BOM Upload document name Returns: dict: Analysis results or creation summary """ doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname) if not doc.bom_file: frappe.throw(_("Please attach a BOM Excel file first.")) if not doc.project: frappe.throw(_("Please select a Project first.")) # Validate machine_code (required for new uploads) machine_code = doc.machine_code if not machine_code: frappe.throw(_("Machine Code is required for BOM Upload. # ==================== Step 2: Ensure Items ====================

def ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, counters=None):
    """
    Walk tree and create Item masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items).
    Must run BEFORE Component Master creation since item_code is a Link field.
    Uses ensure_item_exists() from existing BOM Upload module (idempotent).

    Args:
        tree: List of root nodes
        ws: Excel worksheet
        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
        counters: dict to track {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0}

    Returns:
        dict: counters
    """
    if counters is None:
        counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0}

    for node in tree:
        result = ensure_item_exists(
            node["item_code"],
            node.get("description"),
            node.get("extended_description"),
            node.get("uom"),
            node.get("row_num"),
            ws,
            image_loader,
            node.get("material"),
            node.get("treatment"),
            node.get("weight"),
            node.get("part_number"),
            node.get("manufacturer"),
            node.get("revision")
        )
        counters[result] = counters.get(result, 0) + 1

        if node.get("children"):
            ensure_items_for_all_nodes(node["children"], ws, image_loader, counters)

    return counters


# ==================== Step 3: Create Component Masters ====================

def create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, project, machine_code):
    """
    Create Component Masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf/RM items).
    For existing CMs: apply make/buy merge logic (Excel wins if present, else keep existing).
    For new CMs: set defaults based on item type.

    Business Rules:
    - All items default to "Buy" (conservative approach)
    - T, Y, E prefixed items: released_for_procurement = "No"
    - Other items: released_for_procurement = "Yes"
    - machine_code is set on all Component Masters (enables per-machine tracking)

    Args:
        tree: List of root nodes
        project: Project name
        machine_code: Machine code from BOM Upload (e.g., P0000000003033)

    Returns:
        dict: {"created": X, "existing": X, "updated": X, "failed": X}
    """
    counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
    all_nodes = _get_all_nodes(tree)

    for node in all_nodes:
        item_code = node["item_code"]
        is_assembly = bool(node.get("children"))

        # Check if Component Master already exists (project + item_code + machine_code unique)
        existing = frappe.db.exists(
            "Project Component Master",
            {
                "project": project,
                "item_code": item_code,
                "machine_code": machine_code
            },
        )

        if existing:
            # Merge logic: update make_or_buy only if Excel has a value
            updated = _merge_make_or_buy(existing, node)
            if updated:
                counters["updated"] += 1
            else:
                counters["existing"] += 1

def _merge_make_or_buy(cm_name, node):
    """
    Merge make/buy from Excel into existing Component Master.
    Only updates if Excel has an explicit value; otherwise keeps existing.

    This allows PE2 team to only tag new items or changes in the Excel.
    Previously tagged items can be left blank.

    Returns:
        bool: True if value was updated, False if unchanged
    """
    excel_make_or_buy = node.get("make_or_buy")
    if not excel_make_or_buy:
        return False  # No value in Excel, keep existing

    current_value = frappe.db.get_value(
        "Project Component Master", cm_name, "make_or_buy"
    )

    if current_value != excel_make_or_buy:
        frappe.db.set_value(
            "Project Component Master", cm_name,
            "make_or_buy", excel_make_or_buy
        )
        return True
    return False


def _get_all_nodes(tree):
    """Flatten tree to get ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)."""
    all_nodes = []
    for node in tree:
        all_nodes.append(node)
        if node.get("children"):
            all_nodes.extend(_get_all_nodes(node["children"]))
    return all_nodes


def _get_assembly_nodes(tree):
    """Flatten tree to get all assembly nodes (nodes that have children)."""
    assemblies = []
    for node in tree:
        if node.get("children"):
            assemblies.append(node)
            assemblies.extend(_get_assembly_nodes(node["children"]))
    return assemblies


def filter_tree_by_g_code_state(tree):
    """
    Filter tree to remove G-code items with non-released STATE.

    Business Rule (Approach 1 - G-level only):
    - If item starts with "G" and STATE != "RELEASED" (or blank), skip entire subtree
    - This prevents creation of Items, Component Masters, and BOMs for unreleased designs

    Args:
        tree: List of root nodes from build_tree()

    Returns:
        tuple: (filtered_tree, skipped_info)
            filtered_tree: Tree with non-released G-codes removed
            skipped_info: Dict with {"count": N, "items": [...]} for user notification
    """
    filtered_tree = []
    skipped_items = []

    def should_skip_node(node):
        """Check if this G-code node should be skipped."""
        item_code = str(node.get("item_code", "")).upper()

        # Only check G-codes
        if not item_code.startswith("G"):
            return False

        # Get STATE value
        state = node.get("state")

        # Skip if STATE is blank or not "RELEASED"
        if not state or str(state).strip().upper() != "RELEASED":
            return True

        return False

    def count_subtree_nodes(node):
        """Count total nodes in a subtree."""
        count = 1  # Count this node
        if node.get("children"):
            for child in node["children"]:
                count += count_subtree_nodes(child)
        return count

    def filter_recursive(nodes):
        """Recursively filter nodes."""
        filtered = []
        for node in nodes:
            if should_skip_node(node):
                # Skip this G-code and all its children
                item_count = count_subtree_nodes(node)
                skipped_items.append({
                    "item_code": node.get("item_code"),
                    "description": node.get("description"),
                    "state": node.get("state") or "(blank)",
                    "subtree_count": item_count
                })
            else:
                # Keep this node, but filter its children recursively
                filtered_node = node.copy()
                if node.get("children"):
                    filtered_node["children"] = filter_recursive(node["children"])
                filtered.append(filtered_node)
        return filtered

    filtered_tree = filter_recursive(tree)

    skipped_info = {
        "count": len(skipped_items),
        "total_items_skipped": sum(item["subtree_count"] for item in skipped_items),
        "items": skipped_items
    }

    return filtered_tree, skipped_info

"""
Integration Test Suite for BOM Upload → Procurement Validation

Tests the full flow: Excel upload → BOM/CM creation → MR validation.
Uses real bom_upload_enhanced.py to create test data (not manual CM creation).

Test Scenarios:
  1. G-code level MR validation (Decision 20)
  2. Make/Buy enforcement
  3. Cross-machine CM isolation
  4. Cumulative MR qty tracking

Run:
    bench --site clevertech-uat.bharatbodh.com run-tests \
        --app clevertech --module clevertech.tests.test_g_code_validation
"""

import frappe
import openpyxl
import io
from frappe.tests.utils import FrappeTestCase
from frappe.utils.file_manager import save_file


# ============================================================================
# Excel Helper — creates minimal BOM Excel that bom_upload_enhanced can parse
# ============================================================================

def create_test_excel(rows):
    """
    Create a BOM Excel file in memory.

    Args:
        rows: list of dicts with keys:
            item_code, description, qty, level, state (optional)

    Returns:
        bytes: Excel file content
    """
    wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
    ws = wb.active

    # Row 1: Title
    ws["A1"] = "Test BOM Upload"

    # Row 2: Headers (must match bom_upload_enhanced.py dynamic column mapping)
    headers = {
        "A": "Position",
        "C": "Item no",
        "D": "Description",
        "E": "Qty",
        "F": "Rev.",
        "G": "DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA",
        "H": "MATERIAL",
        "I": "Part_number",
        # J = STATE (hardcoded read in parse_rows_dynamic, no header needed)
        "K": "WEIGHT",
        "L": "MANUFACTURER",
        "M": "TIPO_TRATTAMENTO",
        "N": "UM",
        "O": "LivelloBom",
    }
    for col, header in headers.items():
        ws[f"{col}2"] = header

    # Row 3+: Data
    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
        r = i + 3  # Start at row 3
        ws[f"A{r}"] = i + 1                          # Position
        ws[f"C{r}"] = row["item_code"]                # Item no
        ws[f"D{r}"] = row.get("description", "")      # Description
        ws[f"E{r}"] = row.get("qty", 1)               # Qty
        ws[f"F{r}"] = row.get("revision", "01")        # Rev.
        ws[f"G{r}"] = row.get("description", "")      # Extended description
        ws[f"J{r}"] = row.get("state", "")             # STATE (column J, hardcoded)
        ws[f"N{r}"] = row.get("uom", "Nos")           # UM
        ws[f"O{r}"] = row["level"]                     # LivelloBom

    buf = io.BytesIO()
    wb.save(buf)
    return buf.getvalue()


def run_bom_upload(project_name, machine_code, excel_rows):
    """
    Create BOM Upload doc, attach Excel, and run the full upload flow.

    Returns:
        dict: upload result from create_boms_with_validation()
    """
    from clevertech.clevertech.doctype.bom_upload.bom_upload_enhanced import (
        create_boms_with_validation,
    )

    # Create BOM Upload document
    upload_doc = frappe.get_doc({
        "doctype": "BOM Upload",
        "project": project_name,
        "machine_code": machine_code,
    }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

    # Create and attach Excel file
    excel_bytes = create_test_excel(excel_rows)
    file_doc = save_file(
        "test_bom.xlsx",
        excel_bytes,
        "BOM Upload",
        upload_doc.name,
        is_private=1,
    )
    upload_doc.bom_file = file_doc.file_url
    upload_doc.save(ignore_permissions=True)
    frappe.db.commit()

    # Run the upload
    result = create_boms_with_validation(upload_doc.name)
    frappe.db.commit()

    return result, upload_doc.name


# ============================================================================
# Test Data Definitions
# ============================================================================

# Machine 1: MT4000084237
# Hierarchy:
#   MT4000084237 (machine, level 0)
#   ├── GT3000012345 (G-code 1, level 1, RELEASED) — Make
#   │   └── DT0000012345 (raw material, level 2, qty=60) — Buy
#   └── GT3000067890 (G-code 2, level 1, RELEASED) — Make
#       └── DT0000012345 (same raw material, level 2, qty=40) — Buy
#
# After upload:
#   DT0000012345 CM: total_qty_limit = 100, G1 limit = 60, G2 limit = 40

MACHINE_1_CODE = "MT4000084237"
MACHINE_1_ROWS = [
    {"item_code": "MT4000084237", "description": "Test Machine Assembly 1", "qty": 1, "level": 0, "state": "RELEASED"},
    {"item_code": "GT3000012345", "description": "Test G-Code Assembly 1",  "qty": 1, "level": 1, "state": "RELEASED"},
    {"item_code": "DT0000012345", "description": "Test Raw Material Screw", "qty": 60, "level": 2},
    {"item_code": "GT3000067890", "description": "Test G-Code Assembly 2",  "qty": 1, "level": 1, "state": "RELEASED"},
    {"item_code": "DT0000012345", "description": "Test Raw Material Screw", "qty": 40, "level": 2},
]

# Machine 2: MT4000084238 (same raw material, different machine — tests CM isolation)
# Hierarchy:
#   MT4000084238 (machine, level 0)
#   └── GT3000099999 (G-code 3, level 1, RELEASED) — Make
#       └── DT0000012345 (same raw material, level 2, qty=20) — Buy
#
# After upload:
#   DT0000012345 gets a SECOND CM (machine_code=MT4000084238) with limit=20

MACHINE_2_CODE = "MT4000084238"
MACHINE_2_ROWS = [
    {"item_code": "MT4000084238", "description": "Test Machine Assembly 2", "qty": 1, "level": 0, "state": "RELEASED"},
    {"item_code": "GT3000099999", "description": "Test G-Code Assembly 3",  "qty": 1, "level": 1, "state": "RELEASED"},
    {"item_code": "DT0000012345", "description": "Test Raw Material Screw", "qty": 20, "level": 2},
]

RAW_MATERIAL = "DT0000012345"
G_CODE_1 = "GT3000012345"
G_CODE_2 = "GT3000067890"
G_CODE_3 = "GT3000099999"


# ============================================================================
# Test Class
# ============================================================================

class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
    """Integration tests: BOM Upload → MR/PO Validation"""

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

        # --- Project ---
        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Project",
            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
            "status": "Open",
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        # --- Cost Centers (with custom_machine_code for MR filtering) ---
        cls.company = frappe.defaults.get_defaults().company
        root_cc = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Cost Center",
            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1},
            "name",
            order_by="lft asc",
        )

        cls.cost_center_1 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Cost Center",
            "cost_center_name": "Test MT4000084237",
            "company": cls.company,
            "parent_cost_center": root_cc,
            "is_group": 0,
            "custom_machine_code": MACHINE_1_CODE,
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        cls.cost_center_2 = frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Cost Center",
            "cost_center_name": "Test MT4000084238",
            "company": cls.company,
            "parent_cost_center": root_cc,
            "is_group": 0,
            "custom_machine_code": MACHINE_2_CODE,
        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)

        frappe.db.commit()

        # --- BOM Upload: Machine 1 ---
        cls.result_1, cls.upload_1 = run_bom_upload(
            cls.project.name, MACHINE_1_CODE, MACHINE_1_ROWS
        )

        # --- BOM Upload: Machine 2 (same raw material, different machine) ---
        cls.result_2, cls.upload_2 = run_bom_upload(
            cls.project.name, MACHINE_2_CODE, MACHINE_2_ROWS
        )

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        """Clean up all test data"""
        # MRs
        for mr in frappe.db.get_all("Material Request",
                                     {"custom_project_": cls.project.name}, pluck="name"):
            frappe.get_doc("Material Request", mr).cancel()
            frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr, force=True)

        # CMs
        for cm in frappe.db.get_all("Project Component Master",
                                     {"project": cls.project.name}, pluck="name"):
            frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cm, force=True)

        # BOMs (cancel first, then delete)
        test_items = ["MT4000084237", "MT4000084238", "GT3000012345",
                      "GT3000067890", "GT3000099999"]
        for item_code in test_items:
            for bom in frappe.db.get_all("BOM", {"item": item_code, "docstatus": 1}, pluck="name"):
                frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom).cancel()
            for bom in frappe.db.get_all("BOM", {"item": item_code}, pluck="name"):
                frappe.delete_doc("BOM", bom, force=True)

        # BOM Upload docs
        for name in [cls.upload_1, cls.upload_2]:
            if frappe.db.exists("BOM Upload", name):
                frappe.delete_doc("BOM Upload", name, force=True)

        # Items
        all_items = test_items + [RAW_MATERIAL]
        for item_code in all_items:
            if frappe.db.exists("Item", item_code):
                frappe.delete_doc("Item", item_code, force=True)

        # Cost Centers
        for cc in [cls.cost_center_1, cls.cost_center_2]:
            frappe.delete_doc("Cost Center", cc.name, force=True)

        # Project
        frappe.delete_doc("Project", cls.project.name, force=True)

        frappe.db.commit()
        super().tearDownClass()

    def setUp(self):
        frappe.set_user("Administrator")

    def tearDown(self):
        """Delete MRs created during this test"""
        for mr in frappe.db.get_all("Material Request",
                                     {"custom_project_": self.project.name}, pluck="name"):
            try:
                doc = frappe.get_doc("Material Request", mr)
                if doc.docstatus == 1:
                    doc.cancel()
                frappe.delete_doc("Material Request", mr, force=True)
            except Exception:
                pass
        frappe.db.commit()

    # --- Helpers ---

    def _make_mr(self, item_code, qty, machine_code, cost_center, bom_no=None):
        """Create a test Material Request"""
        item_row = {
            "item_code": item_code,
            "qty": qty,
            "schedule_date": frappe.utils.today(),
            "warehouse": "Material Staging - CT",
        }
        if bom_no:
            item_row["bom_no"] = bom_no

        return frappe.get_doc({
            "doctype": "Material Request",
            "material_request_type": "Purchase",
            "custom_project_": self.project.name,
            "custom_machine_code": machine_code,
            "custom_cost_center": cost_center,
            "items": [item_row],
        })

    def _get_bom_for_item(self, item_code):
        """Get the active default BOM for an item"""
        return frappe.db.get_value(
            "BOM", {"item": item_code, "is_active": 1, "is_default": 1, "docstatus": 1}, "name"
        )

    # =========================================================================
    # SETUP VERIFICATION — confirm upload created what we expect
    # =========================================================================

    def test_00_upload_succeeded(self):
        """Verify BOM upload completed successfully"""
        self.assertEqual(self.result_1.get("status"), "success",
                         f"Machine 1 upload failed: {self.result_1}")
        self.assertEqual(self.result_2.get("status"), "success",
                         f"Machine 2 upload failed: {self.result_2}")

    def test_01_cm_created_with_correct_limits(self):
        """Verify CM for raw material has correct total_qty_limit from BOM explosion"""
        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Project Component Master",
            {"project": self.project.name, "item_code": RAW_MATERIAL,
             "machine_code": MACHINE_1_CODE},
            ["total_qty_limit", "bom_qty_required", "make_or_buy"],
            as_dict=True,
        )
        self.assertIsNotNone(cm, "CM not found for raw material on machine 1")
        self.assertEqual(cm.make_or_buy, "Buy")
        # bom_qty_required should be 60 + 40 = 100
        self.assertEqual(cm.bom_qty_required, 100,
                         f"Expected bom_qty_required=100, got {cm.bom_qty_required}")
        # total_qty_limit = max(project_qty=0, bom_qty_required=100) = 100
        self.assertEqual(cm.total_qty_limit, 100,
                         f"Expected total_qty_limit=100, got {cm.total_qty_limit}")

    def test_02_bom_usage_has_g_codes(self):
        """Verify bom_usage rows have g_code populated for G-code validation"""
        cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Project Component Master",
            {"project": self.project.name, "item_code": RAW_MATERIAL,
             "machine_code": MACHINE_1_CODE},
            "name",
        )
        cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm_name)

        g_codes_found = set()
        for row in cm.bom_usage:
            if row.g_code:
                g_codes_found.add(row.g_code)

        self.assertIn(G_CODE_1, g_codes_found,
                       f"G-code {G_CODE_1} not found in bom_usage. Found: {g_codes_found}")
        self.assertIn(G_CODE_2, g_codes_found,
                       f"G-code {G_CODE_2} not found in bom_usage. Found: {g_codes_found}")

    def test_03_cross_machine_creates_separate_cm(self):
        """Same item in 2 machines should have 2 separate CMs"""
        cm_count = frappe.db.count(
            "Project Component Master",
            {"project": self.project.name, "item_code": RAW_MATERIAL},
        )
        self.assertEqual(cm_count, 2, f"Expected 2 CMs for {RAW_MATERIAL}, got {cm_count}")

    # =========================================================================
    # G-CODE LEVEL VALIDATION
    # =========================================================================

    def test_10_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
        """MR with bom_no should use G-code limit (60), not overall (100)"""
        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)
        mr = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 70, MACHINE_1_CODE,
                           self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {G_CODE_1}", error_msg)

    def test_11_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
        """MR without bom_no should use overall CM limit (100)"""
        mr = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 110, MACHINE_1_CODE,
                           self.cost_center_1.name)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn("overall limit", error_msg)

    def test_12_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
        """60 for G1 + 40 for G2 = 100 total. Both should pass (each within own limit)"""
        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)
        bom_g2 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_2)

        mr1 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 60, MACHINE_1_CODE,
                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)
        mr1.insert()

        mr2 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 40, MACHINE_1_CODE,
                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g2)
        mr2.insert()

        self.assertTrue(mr1.name)
        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

    def test_13_cumulative_qty_across_multiple_mrs(self):
        """Second MR for same G-code counts first MR's qty (40 + 30 = 70 > 60)"""
        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)

        mr1 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 40, MACHINE_1_CODE,
                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)
        mr1.insert()

        mr2 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 30, MACHINE_1_CODE,
                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr2.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn(f"G-code {G_CODE_1}", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # MAKE / BUY ENFORCEMENT
    # =========================================================================

    def test_20_make_item_blocked_from_mr(self):
        """G-code items are 'Make' — cannot be added to Material Request"""
        mr = self._make_mr(G_CODE_1, 1, MACHINE_1_CODE, self.cost_center_1.name)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn("Make", error_msg)

    # =========================================================================
    # CROSS-MACHINE CM ISOLATION
    # =========================================================================

    def test_30_cross_machine_limits_are_independent(self):
        """Same item on machine 2 (limit=20) should not share machine 1's limit (100)"""
        bom_g3 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_3)

        # Machine 2: 25 exceeds its limit of 20
        mr = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 25, MACHINE_2_CODE,
                           self.cost_center_2.name, bom_no=bom_g3)

        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
            mr.insert()

        # Should show limit of 20, not 100
        error_msg = str(ctx.exception)
        self.assertIn("20", error_msg)

    def test_31_machine1_mr_does_not_affect_machine2(self):
        """MR on machine 1 should not reduce machine 2's available qty"""
        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)
        bom_g3 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_3)

        # Use 60 on machine 1 (G-code 1's full limit)
        mr1 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 60, MACHINE_1_CODE,
                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)
        mr1.insert()

        # Machine 2: 20 should still pass (its own limit, unaffected by machine 1)
        mr2 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 20, MACHINE_2_CODE,
                            self.cost_center_2.name, bom_no=bom_g3)
        mr2.insert()

        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)

   128→#   MT4000084237 (machine, level 0)
   129→#   ├── GT3000012345 (G-code 1, level 1, RELEASED) — Make
   130→#   │   └── DT0000012345 (raw material, level 2, qty=60) — Buy
   131→#   └── GT3000067890 (G-code 2, level 1, RELEASED) — Make
   132→#       └── DT0000012345 (same raw material, level 2, qty=40) — Buy
   133→#
   134→# After upload:
   135→#   DT0000012345 CM: total_qty_limit = 100, G1 limit = 60, G2 limit = 40
   136→
   137→MACHINE_1_CODE = "MT4000084237"
   138→MACHINE_1_ROWS = [
   139→    {"item_code": "MT4000084237", "description": "Test Machine Assembly 1", "qty": 1, "level": 0, "state": "RELEASED"},
   140→    {"item_code": "GT3000012345", "description": "Test G-Code Assembly 1",  "qty": 1, "level": 1, "state": "RELEASED"},
   141→    {"item_code": "DT0000012345", "description": "Test Raw Material Screw", "qty": 60, "level": 2},
   142→    {"item_code": "GT3000067890", "description": "Test G-Code Assembly 2",  "qty": 1, "level": 1, "state": "RELEASED"},
   143→    {"item_code": "DT0000012345", "description": "Test Raw Material Screw", "qty": 40, "level": 2},
   144→]
   145→
   146→# Machine 2: MT4000084238 (same raw material, different machine — tests CM isolation)
   147→# Hierarchy:
   148→#   MT4000084238 (machine, level 0)
   149→#   └── GT3000099999 (G-code 3, level 1, RELEASED) — Make
   150→#       └── DT0000012345 (same raw material, level 2, qty=20) — Buy
   151→#
   152→# After upload:
   153→#   DT0000012345 gets a SECOND CM (machine_code=MT4000084238) with limit=20
   154→
   155→MACHINE_2_CODE = "MT4000084238"
   156→MACHINE_2_ROWS = [
   157→    {"item_code": "MT4000084238", "description": "Test Machine Assembly 2", "qty": 1, "level": 0, "state": "RELEASED"},
   158→    {"item_code": "GT3000099999", "description": "Test G-Code Assembly 3",  "qty": 1, "level": 1, "state": "RELEASED"},
   159→    {"item_code": "DT0000012345", "description": "Test Raw Material Screw", "qty": 20, "level": 2},
   160→]
   161→
   162→RAW_MATERIAL = "DT0000012345"
   163→G_CODE_1 = "GT3000012345"
   164→G_CODE_2 = "GT3000067890"
   165→G_CODE_3 = "GT3000099999"
   166→
   167→
   168→# ============================================================================
   169→# Test Class
   170→# ============================================================================
   171→
   172→class TestGCodeValidation(FrappeTestCase):
   173→    """Integration tests: BOM Upload → MR/PO Validation"""
   174→
   175→    @classmethod
   176→    def setUpClass(cls):
   177→        super().setUpClass()
   178→        frappe.set_user("Administrator")
   179→
   180→        # --- Project ---
   181→        cls.project = frappe.get_doc({
   182→            "doctype": "Project",
   183→            "project_name": "TEST-G-CODE-VALIDATION",
   184→            "status": "Open",
   185→        }).insert(ignore_permissions=True)
   186→
   187→        # --- Cost Centers (with custom_machine_code for MR filtering) ---
   188→        cls.company = frappe.defaults.get_defaults().company
   189→        root_cc = frappe.db.get_value(
   190→            "Cost Center",
   191→            {"company": cls.company, "is_group": 1},
   192→            "name",
   193→            order_by="lft asc",
   194→        )
   195→
   196→        cls.cost_center_1 = frappe.get_doc({
   197→            "doctype": "Cost Center",

   199→            "company": cls.company,
   200→            "parent_cost_center": root_cc,
   201→            "is_group": 0,
   202→            "custom_machine_code": MACHINE_1_CODE,

   213→
   214→        frappe.db.commit()
   215→
   216→        # --- BOM Upload: Machine 1 ---

   221→        # --- BOM Upload: Machine 2 (same raw material, different machine) ---

   225→
   226→    @classmethod
   227→    def tearDownClass(cls):
   228→        """Clean up all test data"""
   229→        # MRs
   230→        for mr in frappe.db.get_all("Material Request",

   235→        # CMs
   236→        for cm in frappe.db.get_all("Project Component Master",
   237→                                     {"project": cls.project.name}, pluck="name"):
   238→            frappe.delete_doc("Project Component Master", cm, force=True)
   239→
   240→        # BOMs (cancel first, then delete)
   241→        test_items = ["MT4000084237", "MT4000084238", "GT3000012345",
   242→                      "GT3000067890", "GT3000099999"]
   243→        for item_code in test_items:
   244→            for bom in frappe.db.get_all("BOM", {"item": item_code, "docstatus": 1}, pluck="name"):
   245→                frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom).cancel()
   246→            for bom in frappe.db.get_all("BOM", {"item": item_code}, pluck="name"):
   247→                frappe.delete_doc("BOM", bom, force=True)
   248→
   249→        # BOM Upload docs
   250→        for name in [cls.upload_1, cls.upload_2]:
   251→            if frappe.db.exists("BOM Upload", name):
   252→                frappe.delete_doc("BOM Upload", name, force=True)
   253→
   254→        # Items
   255→        all_items = test_items + [RAW_MATERIAL]
   256→        for item_code in all_items:
   257→            if frappe.db.exists("Item", item_code):

   260→        # Cost Centers

   264→        # Project

   266→
   267→        frappe.db.commit()
   268→        super().tearDownClass()
   269→
   270→    def setUp(self):
   271→        frappe.set_user("Administrator")
   272→
   273→    def tearDown(self):
   274→        """Delete MRs created during this test"""
   275→        for mr in frappe.db.get_all("Material Request",
   276→                                     {"custom_project_": self.project.name}, pluck="name"):
   277→            try:
   278→                doc = frappe.get_doc("Material Request", mr)

   283→                pass
   284→        frappe.db.commit()

   286→    # --- Helpers ---
   287→
   288→    def _make_mr(self, item_code, qty, machine_code, cost_center, bom_no=None):
   289→        """Create a test Material Request"""
   290→        item_row = {
   291→            "item_code": item_code,
   292→            "qty": qty,

   303→            "custom_machine_code": machine_code,
   304→            "custom_cost_center": cost_center,
   305→            "items": [item_row],
   306→        })
   307→
   308→    def _get_bom_for_item(self, item_code):
   309→        """Get the active default BOM for an item"""
   310→        return frappe.db.get_value(
   311→            "BOM", {"item": item_code, "is_active": 1, "is_default": 1, "docstatus": 1}, "name"
   312→        )
   313→
   314→    # =========================================================================
   315→    # SETUP VERIFICATION — confirm upload created what we expect
   316→    # =========================================================================
   317→
   318→    def test_00_upload_succeeded(self):
   319→        """Verify BOM upload completed successfully"""
   320→        self.assertEqual(self.result_1.get("status"), "success",
   321→                         f"Machine 1 upload failed: {self.result_1}")
   322→        self.assertEqual(self.result_2.get("status"), "success",

   324→
   325→    def test_01_cm_created_with_correct_limits(self):
   326→        """Verify CM for raw material has correct total_qty_limit from BOM explosion"""
   327→        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
   328→            "Project Component Master",
   329→            {"project": self.project.name, "item_code": RAW_MATERIAL,
   330→             "machine_code": MACHINE_1_CODE},
   331→            ["total_qty_limit", "bom_qty_required", "make_or_buy"],
   332→            as_dict=True,
   333→        )
   334→        self.assertIsNotNone(cm, "CM not found for raw material on machine 1")
   335→        self.assertEqual(cm.make_or_buy, "Buy")
   336→        # bom_qty_required should be 60 + 40 = 100
   337→        self.assertEqual(cm.bom_qty_required, 100,
   338→                         f"Expected bom_qty_required=100, got {cm.bom_qty_required}")
   339→        # total_qty_limit = max(project_qty=0, bom_qty_required=100) = 100
   340→        self.assertEqual(cm.total_qty_limit, 100,
   341→                         f"Expected total_qty_limit=100, got {cm.total_qty_limit}")
   342→
   343→    def test_02_bom_usage_has_g_codes(self):
   344→        """Verify bom_usage rows have g_code populated for G-code validation"""
   345→        cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
   346→            "Project Component Master",
   347→            {"project": self.project.name, "item_code": RAW_MATERIAL,
   348→             "machine_code": MACHINE_1_CODE},
   349→            "name",
   350→        )
   351→        cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm_name)
   352→
   353→        g_codes_found = set()
   354→        for row in cm.bom_usage:
   355→            if row.g_code:
   356→                g_codes_found.add(row.g_code)
   357→
   358→        self.assertIn(G_CODE_1, g_codes_found,
   359→                       f"G-code {G_CODE_1} not found in bom_usage. Found: {g_codes_found}")
   360→        self.assertIn(G_CODE_2, g_codes_found,
   361→                       f"G-code {G_CODE_2} not found in bom_usage. Found: {g_codes_found}")
   362→
   363→    def test_03_cross_machine_creates_separate_cm(self):
   364→        """Same item in 2 machines should have 2 separate CMs"""
   365→        cm_count = frappe.db.count(

   370→
   371→    # =========================================================================
   372→    # G-CODE LEVEL VALIDATION
   373→    # =========================================================================
   374→
   375→    def test_10_mr_with_bom_no_validates_against_g_code_limit(self):
   376→        """MR with bom_no should use G-code limit (60), not overall (100)"""
   377→        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)
   378→        mr = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 70, MACHINE_1_CODE,
   379→                           self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)
   380→
   381→        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:

   386→
   387→    def test_11_mr_without_bom_no_validates_against_overall_limit(self):
   388→        """MR without bom_no should use overall CM limit (100)"""
   389→        mr = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 110, MACHINE_1_CODE,
   390→                           self.cost_center_1.name)
   391→
   392→        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:
   393→            mr.insert()

   397→
   398→    def test_12_g_codes_have_independent_limits(self):
   399→        """60 for G1 + 40 for G2 = 100 total. Both should pass (each within own limit)"""
   400→        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)
   401→        bom_g2 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_2)
   402→
   403→        mr1 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 60, MACHINE_1_CODE,
   404→                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)
   405→        mr1.insert()

   407→        mr2 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 40, MACHINE_1_CODE,

   409→        mr2.insert()

   412→        self.assertTrue(mr2.name)
   413→
   414→    def test_13_cumulative_qty_across_multiple_mrs(self):
   415→        """Second MR for same G-code counts first MR's qty (40 + 30 = 70 > 60)"""
   416→        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)
   417→
   418→        mr1 = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 40, MACHINE_1_CODE,
   419→                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)
   420→        mr1.insert()

   423→                            self.cost_center_1.name, bom_no=bom_g1)
   424→
   425→        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:

   430→
   431→    # =========================================================================
   432→    # MAKE / BUY ENFORCEMENT
   433→    # =========================================================================
   434→
   435→    def test_20_make_item_blocked_from_mr(self):
   436→        """G-code items are 'Make' — cannot be added to Material Request"""
   437→        mr = self._make_mr(G_CODE_1, 1, MACHINE_1_CODE, self.cost_center_1.name)

   439→        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:

   444→
   445→    # =========================================================================
   446→    # CROSS-MACHINE CM ISOLATION

   448→
   449→    def test_30_cross_machine_limits_are_independent(self):
   450→        """Same item on machine 2 (limit=20) should not share machine 1's limit (100)"""
   451→        bom_g3 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_3)
   452→
   453→        # Machine 2: 25 exceeds its limit of 20
   454→        mr = self._make_mr(RAW_MATERIAL, 25, MACHINE_2_CODE,

   456→
   457→        with self.assertRaises(frappe.ValidationError) as ctx:

   463→
   464→    def test_31_machine1_mr_does_not_affect_machine2(self):
   465→        """MR on machine 1 should not reduce machine 2's available qty"""
   466→        bom_g1 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_1)
   467→        bom_g3 = self._get_bom_for_item(G_CODE_3)
   468→
   469→        # Use 60 on machine 1 (G-code 1's full limit)
