file.content: def on_bom_submit(doc, method=None): """ Called when a BOM is submitted. Updates BOM Usage table for all items in the BOM that have Component Masters. Shows info message if this is a project BOM that's not tracked. BOM Version Handling: - If a different BOM was already active for this item+project, the old BOM's bom_usage entries are removed from children before adding the new BOM's entries. - Warns user about the version change and any existing MRs/POs. """ # Store BOM structure hash on the BOM itself (source of truth for change detection) # This runs for ALL submitted BOMs, not just project BOMs bom_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(doc) if bom_hash: doc.db_set('custom_bom_structure_hash', bom_hash, update_modified=False) if not doc.project: # No project linked - skip silently (standard product BOM) return # Check if this BOM itself is tracked in Component Master component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) if not component_master: # Project BOM but not tracked - inform user frappe.msgprint( _("This BOM is linked to project {0} but is not tracked in Project Component Master. Procurement monitoring is not enabled for {1}.").format( doc.project, doc.item ), title=_("BOM Not Tracked for Procurement"), indicator="blue", alert=True ) return # Silent skip for processing # --- Backfill Missing BOM Version History --- # If version history is empty but BOMs exist, backfill them if not component_master.bom_version_history: _backfill_bom_version_history(doc.project, doc.item, doc.name) # Reload Component Master after backfill component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) # --- BOM Version Change Detection --- old_bom = component_master.active_bom if old_bom and old_bom != doc.name: # A different BOM was active — this is a version change _handle_bom_version_change(doc.project, doc.item, old_bom, doc.name) elif not old_bom: # First BOM being linked — add initial version to history _add_initial_bom_version(doc.project, doc.item, doc.name) # Consolidate duplicate items in BOM (sum quantities) # If same item appears multiple times, we need total qty for bom_usage item_quantities = {} for item in doc.items: if item.item_code in item_quantities: item_quantities[item.item_code] += float(item.qty or 0) else: item_quantities[item.item_code] = float(item.qty or 0) # Process consolidated items for item_code, total_qty in item_quantities.items(): add_or_update_bom_usage( project=doc.project, item_code=item_code, parent_bom=doc.name, qty_per_unit=total_qty ) # Update BOM structure hash and related fields for the BOM item itself update_component_master_bom_fields( project=doc.project, item_code=doc.item, bom_name=doc.name ) # Commit all Component Master updates to ensure calculations persist frappe.db.commit()

file.content: frappe.msgprint( msg=f"Successfully recalculated quantities for {affected_count} child component(s).", title="Recalculation Complete", indicator="green" ) def calculate_bom_qty_required(self): """ Calculate BOM quantity required by summing from all BOMs using this component. Multiplies qty_per_unit by parent's total_qty_limit for correct totals. Also populates total_qty_required on each bom_usage row. IMPORTANT: Only counts parents where make_or_buy = "Make". If parent is "Buy" (procured as unit), this component is covered by parent's procurement and does NOT need separate procurement. For root assemblies (no bom_usage): bom_qty_required = project_qty For loose items "Pending Conversion" (no bom_usage): bom_qty_required = 0 For components in BOMs (has bom_usage): SUM(parent.total_qty_limit × qty_per_unit) where parent.make_or_buy = "Make" Note: Uses total_qty_limit instead of project_qty to handle multi-level hierarchies. For level 1 components, total_qty_limit = project_qty. For level 2+ components, total_qty_limit = MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required). """ if not self.bom_usage: # Root assembly or loose item - no BOM usage rows if self.is_loose_item: self.bom_qty_required = 0 else: self.bom_qty_required = self.project_qty or 0 return # Has BOM usage rows — calculate from all parent BOMs where parent is "Make" total = 0 for usage in self.bom_usage: # Get parent's total quantity limit and make/buy flag parent = frappe.db.get_value( "Project Component Master", {"project": self.project, "item_code": usage.parent_item}, ["total_qty_limit", "make_or_buy"], as_dict=True ) if parent and parent.make_or_buy == "Make": # Parent is assembled in-house — this component needs separate procurement usage.total_qty_required = (usage.qty_per_unit or 0) * (parent.total_qty_limit or 0) total += usage.total_qty_required else: # Parent is "Buy" — this component is covered by parent's procurement usage.total_qty_required = 0 self.bom_qty_required = total def recalculate_children_bom_qty(self, recursive=False, _depth=0, _processed=None): """ When make_or_buy changes on this component, recalculate bom_qty_required for all children in this component's active BOM. If this was changed from Buy→Make, children now need separate procurement. If this was changed from Make→Buy, children are now covered by this component's procurement. Also updates bom_usage table from current BOM (self-healing). Args: recursive: If True, recursively recalculate all descendants in the BOM hierarchy _depth: Internal parameter to track recursion depth (max 10 levels) _processed: Internal parameter to track processed items (avoid infinite loops) Returns: int: Number of child components affected """ # Safety: Prevent infinite recursion MAX_DEPTH = 10 if _depth > MAX_DEPTH: frappe.log_error( title=f"Max recursion depth reached for {self.item_code}", message=f"Stopped at depth {_depth} to prevent infinite recursion" ) return 0

# Copyright (c) 2026, Bharatbodh and contributors # For license information, please see license.txt import frappe from frappe.model.document import Document class ProjectComponentMaster(Document): def before_save(self): """Calculate auto-fields before saving.""" # Detect make_or_buy change and cascade to children if not self.is_new() and self.has_value_changed("make_or_buy"): self.flags.make_or_buy_changed = True # Validate: Warn if changing to "Make" but all parents are "Buy" if self.make_or_buy == "Make" and self.bom_usage: self._validate_make_with_buy_parents() self.calculate_bom_qty_required() self.calculate_total_qty_limit() self.calculate_procurement_totals() self.update_procurement_status() self.calculate_budgeted_rate_rollup() self.update_bom_conversion_status() def _validate_make_with_buy_parents(self): """ Validate that changing to "Make" makes sense given parent's Make/Buy status. If all parents are "Buy", then changing this component to "Make" has no effect on procurement quantities (children will still have zero requirement). """ # Check all parents' Make/Buy status parent_statuses = [] for usage in self.bom_usage: parent = frappe.db.get_value( "Project Component Master", {"project": self.project, "item_code": usage.parent_item}, ["make_or_buy", "name"], as_dict=True ) if parent: parent_statuses.append({ "item": usage.parent_item, "make_or_buy": parent.make_or_buy, "name": parent.name }) # Check if all parents are "Buy" all_parents_buy = all(p["make_or_buy"] == "Buy" for p in parent_statuses if p["make_or_buy"]) if all_parents_buy and parent_statuses: # Build warning message with parent details parent_list = "
".join([ f"• {p['item']} (Make/Buy: {p['make_or_buy']})" for p in parent_statuses ]) frappe.msgprint( msg=f"Warning: You are changing {self.item_code} to Make, " f"but all its parent assemblies are set to Buy:

" f"{parent_list}

" f"Impact: This component and its children will have zero procurement quantities " f"because they are covered by the parent's procurement.

" f"Recommendation: If you want to procure this component and its children separately, " f"change the parent assembly to Make first, then change this component to Make.", title="Make/Buy Configuration Warning", indicator="orange" ) def on_update(self): """After save, cascade recalculation if make_or_buy changed.""" if getattr(self.flags, "make_or_buy_changed", False): # Show message to user about recursive recalculation old_value = self.get_doc_before_save().make_or_buy if not self.is_new() else None new_value = self.make_or_buy if old_value != new_value: frappe.msgprint( msg=f"Make/Buy changed from {old_value or 'None'} to {new_value} for {self.item_code}.
" f"Triggering recursive recalculation for all child components in the BOM hierarchy...", title="Recalculating Child Components", indicator="blue" ) # Trigger recursive recalculation affected_count = self.recalculate_children_bom_qty(recursive=True, _depth=0) if affected_count > 0: # Check if this component has zero total_qty_limit (indicates parent is "Buy") if self.total_qty_limit == 0 and new_value == "Make": frappe.msgprint( msg=f"Recalculated {affected_count} child component(s).

" f"Note: All quantities set to zero because parent assembly is Buy. " f"Child procurement is covered by parent's procurement.", title="Recalculation Complete", indicator="orange" ) else: frappe.msgprint(

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture:

1. **before_save** detects make_or_buy change and sets flag
2. **on_update** triggers `recalculate_children_bom_qty(recursive=True)` which cascades to all children

So when we call `_change_d_codes_to_make()` and do `cm.save()`, the on_update hook DOES cascade to children. This means RM's total_qty_limit should be recalculated.

But wait — the `_change_d_codes_to_make()` saves each D-code CM. When D-code's make_or_buy changes from Buy to Make:


1. before_save flags the change and recalculates the D-code's own BOM qty
2. on_update then cascades the recalculation down to all RMs

The issue is that D-code's total_qty_limit depends on its parent G-code being correct first. If G-code's total_qty_limit is accurate, then D-code's recalculation should propagate properly through the chain.

Let me work through the math: M-code has no BOM usage so its total_qty_limit is 0, which means G-code's bom_qty_required becomes 1 × 0 = 0, giving G-code a total_qty_limit of max(1, 0) = 1. Then D-code, with G-code as its parent, should calculate bom_qty_required as 1 × 1 = 1, resulting in a total_qty_limit of max(0, 1) = 1.

Following that logic, RM should have a total bom_qty_required of 100 (60 from D-code 1 plus 40 from D-code 2), giving it a total_qty_limit of 100. But the test is showing D-code with total_qty_limit = 0, which suggests the recalculation order might be processing components alphabetically and causing the parent limits to be computed before their children are ready. - DT0000054321 (D-code): Processed, but G-code hasn't been recalculated yet → sees G-code total_qty_limit as stale (0?) → bom_qty_required = 0
- GT3000012345 (G-code): Processed, sees M-code total_qty_limit → M-code project_qty=0, total_qty_limit=0 → bom_qty_required = 0, total_qty_limit = max(1, 0) = 1
- MT4000084237 (M-code): Processed, no bom_usage → total_qty_limit = 0
- RM0000012345 (RM): Processed, parent D-code total_qty_limit = 0 → bom_qty_required = 0

So the recalculation runs in creation order by CM sequence number, which follows the BOM upload tree order (M-code first, then G-code, D-code, RM). This means M-code gets processed with stale limits, then G-code sees the M-code's zero limit, creating a cascading effect where everything ends up with zero quantities.

The issue is that the recalculate function uses `frappe.db.set_value()` to persist each CM's result immediately, so when G-code is processed and calculates total_qty_limit = 1, that value is written to the database. But by then, D-code has already been processed with the old G-code limit.

The real problem is the query order—`frappe.get_all` defaults to sorting by `modified desc`, so the most recently modified CMs come first. If the recalculate runs right after a BOM upload, the CMs might be processed in reverse tree order, which breaks the dependency chain. I should step back and trace through what actually happens when BOMs are submitted in sequence—maybe the recalculate function is being called multiple times and fixing itself, or there's something about the on_submit hooks I'm missing. Let me write a diagnostic script to capture the actual CM values after the upload completes, which will show me exactly what the system is producing rather than guessing at the order of operations.

1→# Copyright (c) 2026, Bharatbodh and contributors 2→# For license information, please see license.txt 3→ 4→import frappe 5→from frappe.model.document import Document 6→ 7→ 8→class ProjectComponentMaster(Document): 9→ 10→ def before_save(self): 11→ """Calculate auto-fields before saving.""" 12→ # Detect make_or_buy change and cascade to children 13→ if not self.is_new() and self.has_value_changed("make_or_buy"): 14→ self.flags.make_or_buy_changed = True 15→ 16→ # Validate: Warn if changing to "Make" but all parents are "Buy" 17→ if self.make_or_buy == "Make" and self.bom_usage: 18→ self._validate_make_with_buy_parents() 19→ 20→ self.calculate_bom_qty_required() 21→ self.calculate_total_qty_limit() 22→ self.calculate_procurement_totals() 23→ self.update_procurement_status() 24→ self.calculate_budgeted_rate_rollup() 25→ self.update_bom_conversion_status() 26→ 27→ def _validate_make_with_buy_parents(self): 28→ """ 29→ Validate that changing to "Make" makes sense given parent's Make/Buy status. 30→ If all parents are "Buy", then changing this component to "Make" has no effect 31→ on procurement quantities (children will still have zero requirement). 32→ """ 33→ # Check all parents' Make/Buy status 34→ parent_statuses = [] 35→ for usage in self.bom_usage: 36→ parent = frappe.db.get_value( 37→ "Project Component Master", 38→ {"project": self.project, "item_code": usage.parent_item}, 39→ ["make_or_buy", "name"], 40→ as_dict=True 41→ ) 42→ if parent: 43→ parent_statuses.append({ 44→ "item": usage.parent_item, 45→ "make_or_buy": parent.make_or_buy, 46→ "name": parent.name 47→ }) 48→ 49→ # Check if all parents are "Buy" 50→ all_parents_buy = all(p["make_or_buy"] == "Buy" for p in parent_statuses if p["make_or_buy"]) 51→ 52→ if all_parents_buy and parent_statuses: 53→ # Build warning message with parent details 54→ parent_list = "
".join([ 55→ f"• {p['item']} (Make/Buy: {p['make_or_buy']})" 56→ for p in parent_statuses 57→ ]) 58→ 59→ frappe.msgprint( 60→ msg=f"Warning: You are changing {self.item_code} to Make, " 61→ f"but all its parent assemblies are set to Buy:

" 62→ f"{parent_list}

" 63→ f"Impact: This component and its children will have zero procurement quantities " 64→ f"because they are covered by the parent's procurement.

" 65→ f"Recommendation: If you want to procure this component and its children separately, " 66→ f"change the parent assembly to Make first, then change this component to Make.", 67→ title="Make/Buy Configuration Warning", 68→ indicator="orange" 69→ ) 70→ 71→ def on_update(self): 72→ """After save, cascade recalculation if make_or_buy changed.""" 73→ if getattr(self.flags, "make_or_buy_changed", False): 74→ # Show message to user about recursive recalculation 75→ old_value = self.get_doc_before_save().make_or_buy if not self.is_new() else None 76→ new_value = self.make_or_buy 77→ 78→ if old_value != new_value: 79→ frappe.msgprint( 80→ msg=f"Make/Buy changed from {old_value or 'None'} to {new_value} for {self.item_code}.
" 81→ f"Triggering recursive recalculation for all child components in the BOM hierarchy...", 82→ title="Recalculating Child Components", 83→ indicator="blue" 84→ ) 85→ 86→ # Trigger recursive recalculation 87→ affected_count = self.recalculate_children_bom_qty(recursive=True, _depth=0) 88→ 89→ if affected_count > 0: 90→ # Check if this component has zero total_qty_limit (indicates parent is "Buy") 91→ if self.total_qty_limit == 0 and new_value == "Make": 92→ frappe.msgprint( 93→ msg=f"Recalculated {affected_count} child component(s).

" 94→ f"Note: All quantities set to zero because parent assembly is Buy. " 95→ f"Child procurement is covered by parent's procurement.", 96→ title="Recalculation Complete", 97→ indicator="orange" 98→ ) 99→ else: 100→ frappe.msgprint( 101→ msg=f"Successfully recalculated quantities for {affected_count} child component(s).", 102→ title="Recalculation Complete", 103→ indicator="green" 104→ ) 105→ 106→ def calculate_bom_qty_required(self): 107→ """ 108→ Calculate BOM quantity required by summing from all BOMs using this component. 109→ Multiplies qty_per_unit by parent's total_qty_limit for correct totals. 110→ Also populates total_qty_required on each bom_usage row. 111→ 112→ IMPORTANT: Only counts parents where make_or_buy = "Make". 113→ If parent is "Buy" (procured as unit), this component is covered by parent's 114→ procurement and does NOT need separate procurement. 115→ 116→ For root assemblies (no bom_usage): bom_qty_required = project_qty 117→ For loose items "Pending Conversion" (no bom_usage): bom_qty_required = 0 118→ For components in BOMs (has bom_usage): SUM(parent.total_qty_limit × qty_per_unit) 119→ where parent.make_or_buy = "Make" 120→ 121→ Note: Uses total_qty_limit instead of project_qty to handle multi-level hierarchies. 122→ For level 1 components, total_qty_limit = project_qty. 123→ For level 2+ components, total_qty_limit = MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required). 124→ """ 125→ if not self.bom_usage: 126→ # Root assembly or loose item - no BOM usage rows 127→ if self.is_loose_item: 128→ self.bom_qty_required = 0 129→ else: 130→ self.bom_qty_required = self.project_qty or 0 131→ return 132→ 133→ # Has BOM usage rows — calculate from all parent BOMs where parent is "Make" 134→ total = 0 135→ for usage in self.bom_usage: 136→ # Get parent's total quantity limit and make/buy flag 137→ parent = frappe.db.get_value( 138→ "Project Component Master", 139→ {"project": self.project, "item_code": usage.parent_item}, 140→ ["total_qty_limit", "make_or_buy"], 141→ as_dict=True 142→ ) 143→ 144→ if parent and parent.make_or_buy == "Make": 145→ # Parent is assembled in-house — this component needs separate procurement 146→ usage.total_qty_required = (usage.qty_per_unit or 0) * (parent.total_qty_limit or 0) 147→ total += usage.total_qty_required 148→ else: 149→ # Parent is "Buy" — this component is covered by parent's procurement 150→ usage.total_qty_required = 0 151→ 152→ self.bom_qty_required = total 153→ 154→ def recalculate_children_bom_qty(self, recursive=False, _depth=0, _processed=None): 155→ """ 156→ When make_or_buy changes on this component, recalculate bom_qty_required 157→ for all children in this component's active BOM. 158→ 159→ If this was changed from Buy→Make, children now need separate procurement. 160→ If this was changed from Make→Buy, children are now covered by this component's procurement. 161→ 162→ Also updates bom_usage table from current BOM (self-healing). 163→ 164→ Args: 165→ recursive: If True, recursively recalculate all descendants in the BOM hierarchy 166→ _depth: Internal parameter to track recursion depth (max 10 levels) 167→ _processed: Internal parameter to track processed items (avoid infinite loops) 168→ 169→ Returns: 170→ int: Number of child components affected 171→ """ 172→ # Safety: Prevent infinite recursion 173→ MAX_DEPTH = 10 174→ if _depth > MAX_DEPTH: 175→ frappe.log_error( 176→ title=f"Max recursion depth reached for {self.item_code}", 177→ message=f"Stopped at depth {_depth} to prevent infinite recursion" 178→ ) 179→ return 0 180→ 181→ if not self.active_bom: 182→ return 0 183→ 184→ # Track processed items to avoid circular references 185→ if _processed is None: 186→ _processed = set() 187→ 188→ if self.name in _processed: 189→ return 0 190→ 191→ _processed.add(self.name) 192→ 193→ # Get BOM and consolidate duplicate items (same as on_bom_submit) 194→ bom = frappe.get_doc("BOM", self.active_bom) 195→ item_quantities = {} 196→ for item in bom.items: 197→ if item.item_code in item_quantities: 198→ item_quantities[item.item_code] += float(item.qty or 0) 199→ else: 200→ item_quantities[item.item_code] = float(item.qty or 0) 201→ 202→ affected_count = 0 203→ 204→ # Update bom_usage and recalculate for each child 205→ for item_code, total_qty in item_quantities.items(): 206→ child_cm_name = frappe.db.get_value( 207→ "Project Component Master", 208→ {"project": self.project, "item_code": item_code}, 209→ "name" 210→ ) 211→ if child_cm_name: 212→ child_doc = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", child_cm_name) 213→ 214→ # Update bom_usage entry with current BOM data 215→ usage_updated = False 216→ for usage in child_doc.bom_usage: 217→ if usage.parent_bom == self.active_bom: 218→ if usage.qty_per_unit != total_qty: 219→ usage.qty_per_unit = total_qty 220→ usage_updated = True 221→ break 222→ 223→ # If bom_usage was updated, save it 224→ if usage_updated: 225→ child_doc.flags.ignore_validate = True 226→ child_doc.save(ignore_permissions=True) 227→ 228→ # Calculate all quantities 229→ child_doc.calculate_bom_qty_required() 230→ child_doc.calculate_total_qty_limit() 231→ child_doc.calculate_procurement_totals() 232→ child_doc.update_procurement_status() 233→ 234→ # Use frappe.db.set_value to persist calculated fields (bypasses save() in hooks) 235→ frappe.db.set_value(child_doc.doctype, child_doc.name, { 236→ "bom_qty_required": child_doc.bom_qty_required, 237→ "total_qty_limit": child_doc.total_qty_limit, 238→ "total_qty_procured": child_doc.total_qty_procured, 239→ "procurement_balance": child_doc.procurement_balance, 240→ "procurement_status": child_doc.procurement_status 241→ }, update_modified=False) 242→ 243→ affected_count += 1 244→ 245→ # Recursive call: If this child also has a BOM and make_or_buy is "Make", 246→ # recalculate its children as well 247→ if recursive and child_doc.has_bom and child_doc.active_bom: 248→ child_affected = child_doc.recalculate_children_bom_qty( 249→ recursive=True, 250→ _depth=_depth + 1, 251→ _processed=_processed 252→ ) 253→ affected_count += child_affected 254→ 255→ # Commit all changes (only at root level) 256→ if _depth == 0: 257→ frappe.db.commit() 258→ 259→ return affected_count 260→ 261→ def calculate_total_qty_limit(self): 262→ """ 263→ Calculate total quantity limit: MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required). 264→ 265→ This is the hard procurement limit enforced in Material Request validation. 266→ - project_qty: Manually entered by user (from Excel or manual entry) 267→ - bom_qty_required: Auto-calculated from BOM usage 268→ - Limit is the maximum of these two values 269→ """ 270→ project = self.project_qty or 0 271→ bom = self.bom_qty_required or 0 272→ self.total_qty_limit = max(project, bom) 273→ 274→ def calculate_procurement_totals(self): 275→ """Sum all procurement records (MR and PO quantities).""" 276→ total = 0 277→ for record in self.procurement_records: 278→ if record.document_type in ("Material Request", "Purchase Order"): 279→ total += record.quantity or 0 280→ self.total_qty_procured = total 281→ self.procurement_balance = (self.total_qty_limit or 0) - total 282→ 283→ def update_procurement_status(self): 284→ """Auto-set status based on quantities.""" 285→ if not self.total_qty_procured: 286→ self.procurement_status = "Not Started" 287→ elif self.total_qty_procured > (self.total_qty_limit or 0): 288→ self.procurement_status = "Over-procured" 289→ elif self.total_qty_procured >= (self.total_qty_limit or 0): 290→ self.procurement_status = "Completed" 291→ else: 292→ self.procurement_status = "In Progress" 293→ 294→ def calculate_budgeted_rate_rollup(self): 295→ """Roll up budgets from child components.""" 296→ if not self.has_bom or not self.active_bom: 297→ return 298→ 299→ try: 300→ bom = frappe.get_doc("BOM", self.active_bom) 301→ except frappe.DoesNotExistError: 302→ return 303→ 304→ total = 0 305→ for item in bom.items: 306→ child_budget = frappe.db.get_value( 307→ "Project Component Master", 308→ {"project": self.project, "item_code": item.item_code}, 309→ "budgeted_rate" 310→ ) 311→ if child_budget: 312→ total += child_budget * item.qty 313→ else: 314→ last_rate = frappe.db.get_value( 315→ "Item", item.item_code, "last_purchase_rate" 316→ ) or 0 317→ total += last_rate * item.qty 318→ 319→ self.budgeted_rate_calculated = total 320→ 321→ def update_bom_conversion_status(self): 322→ """Update conversion status for loose items.""" 323→ if not self.is_loose_item: 324→ self.bom_conversion_status = "Not Applicable" 325→ return 326→ 327→ if not self.can_be_converted_to_bom: 328→ self.bom_conversion_status = "Pending Conversion" 329→ elif len(self.bom_usage) == 0: 330→ self.bom_conversion_status = "Pending Conversion" 331→ elif len(self.bom_usage) == 1: 332→ self.bom_conversion_status = "Converted to BOM" 333→ else: 334→ self.bom_conversion_status = "Partial" 335→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→""" 2→Enhanced BOM Upload with Validation and Component Master Integration 3→ 4→New "Create BOMs with Validation" button on BOM Upload form. 5→Imports existing functions from bom_upload.py — does NOT modify that file. 6→ 7→Sequence (Decision 11 & 12): 8→ 1. Parse Excel (reuse parse_rows, build_tree) 9→ 2. Create Items for assemblies (reuse ensure_item_exists) 10→ 3. Create Component Masters FIRST (active_bom=null) 11→ 4. Analyze upload — hash comparison, blocking checks 12→ 5. Create BOMs bottom-up (reuse create_bom_recursive) 13→ 6. Link active_bom back to Component Masters 14→ 7. BOM on_submit hooks auto-populate BOM Usage tables 15→""" 16→ 17→import frappe 18→from frappe import _ 19→import openpyxl 20→import io 21→import hashlib 22→import json 23→ 24→from clevertech.clevertech.doctype.bom_upload.bom_upload import ( 25→ parse_rows, # Original - will be replaced by parse_rows_dynamic for enhanced upload 26→ build_tree, 27→ ensure_item_exists, 28→ create_bom_recursive, 29→ HAS_IMAGE_LOADER, 30→ # Utility functions (Decision 12A - reuse from original) 31→ clean_code, 32→ to_float, 33→ normalize_uom, 34→ # Mapping functions (Decision 12A - auto-inherit when migrated to doctypes) 35→ normalize_material, 36→ get_surface_treatment, 37→ get_type_of_material, 38→ get_item_group_and_hsn, 39→) 40→ 41→# Conditional import for image loader (same as bom_upload.py) 42→try: 43→ from openpyxl_image_loader import SheetImageLoader 44→except ImportError: 45→ SheetImageLoader = None 46→ 47→from clevertech.project_component_master.bom_hooks import ( 48→ calculate_bom_structure_hash as calculate_bom_doc_hash, 49→) 50→ 51→from clevertech.project_component_master.bulk_generation import ( 52→ populate_bom_usage_tables, 53→) 54→ 55→ 56→# ==================== Dynamic Column Mapping (Phase 4H) ==================== 57→ 58→def map_excel_columns(ws): 59→ """ 60→ Dynamically map Excel column letters to field names by searching row 2 headers. 61→ Handles both old and new PE2 export formats where columns may shift. 62→ 63→ Root Cause (2026-02-01): PE2 export format changed, LivelloBom moved from AR to AQ. 64→ Hardcoded column AR in parse_rows() caused wrong hierarchy (raw materials got BOMs). 65→ 66→ Args: 67→ ws: Excel worksheet 68→ 69→ Returns: 70→ dict: Mapping of field names to column letters 71→ Example: {"item_code": "C", "level": "AQ", ...} 72→ 73→ Raises: 74→ frappe.ValidationError: If any required column header is not found 75→ """ 76→ # Define required column headers (exact matches from Excel row 2) 77→ # Based on RCA 2026-02-01: All headers consistent except LivelloBom position 78→ header_mapping = { 79→ # Field name: [List of possible header values in row 2] 80→ "position": ["Position"], 81→ "item_code": ["Item no"], 82→ "description": ["Description"], 83→ "qty": ["Qty"], 84→ "revision": ["Rev."], 85→ "extended_description": ["DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA"], 86→ "material": ["MATERIAL"], 87→ "part_number": ["Part_number"], 88→ "weight": ["WEIGHT"], 89→ "manufacturer": ["MANUFACTURER"], 90→ "treatment": ["TIPO_TRATTAMENTO"], 91→ "uom": ["UM"], 92→ "level": ["LivelloBom"], # Map each required field to its column 108→ for field_name, possible_headers in header_mapping.items(): 109→ found = False 110→ for col_letter, header_val in headers_in_excel.items(): 111→ if header_val in possible_headers: 112→ found_columns[field_name] = col_letter 113→ found = True 114→ break 115→ 116→ if not found: 117→ missing_columns.append({ 118→ "field": field_name, 119→ "expected_headers": possible_headers 120→ }) 121→ 122→ # Missing required columns:") 125→ error_msg += "\n\n" 126→ for missing in missing_columns: 127→ error_msg += f"• Field: {missing['field']}\n" 128→ error_msg += f" Expected header: {', '.join(missing['expected_headers'])}\n\n" 129→ 130→ error_msg += f"\nFound headers in row 2:\n" 131→ for col, header in sorted(headers_in_excel.items())[:30]: # Show first 30 columns 132→ error_msg += f" Column {col}: {header}\n" 133→ 134→ frappe.throw(error_msg, title=_("Invalid Excel Format")) 135→ 136→ return found_columns 137→ 138→ 139→def parse_rows_dynamic(ws): 140→ """ 141→ Parse Excel rows using dynamic column mapping (handles format changes). 142→ Replacement for hardcoded parse_rows() from bom_upload.py (Phase 4H). 143→ 144→ Uses map_excel_columns() to find column positions by header name instead of 145→ hardcoded positions. This handles PE2 export format changes automatically. 146→ 147→ Args: 148→ ws: Excel worksheet 149→ 150→ Returns: 151→ list: List of row dicts with parsed data (same format as parse_rows) 152→ """ 153→ # Get dynamic column mapping (validates required columns exist) 154→ col_map = map_excel_columns(ws) 155→ 156→ rows = [] 157→ 158→ # Start from row 3 (row 1 = title, row 2 = headers, row 3+ = data) 159→ for r in range(3, ws.max_row + 1): 160→ # Check if item_code exists (required field) 161→ code_raw = ws[f"{col_map['item_code']}{r}"].value 162→ if not code_raw: 163→ continue # Skip empty rows 164→ 165→ # Read position (column A or B fallback, same as original) 166→ position_col = col_map.get("position", "A") 167→ position = ws[f"{position_col}{r}"].value or ws[f"B{r}"].value 168→ 169→ rows.append({ 170→ "row_num": r, # Store row number for image extraction 171→ "position": position, 172→ "item_code": clean_code(code_raw), 173→ "description": ws[f"{col_map['description']}{r}"].value, 174→ "extended_description": ws[f"{col_map['extended_description']}{r}"].value, 175→ "qty": to_float(ws[f"{col_map['qty']}{r}"].value, 1), 176→ "revision": ws[f"{col_map['revision']}{r}"].value, 177→ "material": ws[f"{col_map['material']}{r}"].value, 178→ "part_number": ws[f"{col_map['part_number']}{r}"].value, 179→ "weight": to_float(ws[f"{col_map['weight']}{r}"].value, 0), 180→ "manufacturer": ws[f"{col_map['manufacturer']}{r}"].value, 181→ "treatment": ws[f"{col_map['treatment']}{r}"].value, 182→ "uom": normalize_uom(ws[f"{col_map['uom']}{r}"].value), 183→ "level": int(ws[f"{col_map['level']}{r}"].value or 0), # CRITICAL! Please enter the machine code (e.g., P0000000003033).")) 216→ 217→ # Step 1: Parse Excel (reuse existing logic) 218→ file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file}) 219→ content = file_doc.get_content() 220→ 221→ wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True) 222→ ws = wb.active 223→ 224→ # Initialize image loader only if available 225→ image_loader = None 226→ if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and SheetImageLoader: 227→ try: 228→ image_loader = SheetImageLoader(ws) 229→ except Exception: 230→ pass # Image loading optional 231→ 232→ rows = parse_rows_dynamic(ws) # Phase 4H: Dynamic column mapping 233→ tree = build_tree(rows) 234→ 235→ if not tree: 236→ frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file.")) 237→ 238→ # Filter tree to remove non-released G-codes (STATE != "RELEASED") 239→ filtered_tree, skipped_info = filter_tree_by_g_code_state(tree) 240→ 241→ # Notify user if any G-codes were skipped 242→ if skipped_info["count"] > 0: 243→ skip_details = "
".join([ 244→ f"• {item['item_code']} ({item['description'][:50]}...) - STATE: {item['state']} - Skipped {item['subtree_count']} items" 245→ for item in skipped_info["items"] 246→ ]) 247→ frappe.msgprint( 248→ _( 249→ f"Skipped {skipped_info['count']} non-released G-code(s)

T, Y, E items get released_for_procurement=No. 272→ # (active_bom=null at this stage for assemblies, set later after BOM creation) 273→ cm_counters = create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, doc.project, machine_code) 274→ 275→ # Step 4: Analyze upload (now Component Masters exist for blocking checks) 276→ analysis = analyze_upload(tree, doc.project, machine_code) 277→ 278→ # Step 5: Check for blocking issues 279→ if analysis["loose_blocked"]: 280→ return { 281→ "status": "blocked", 282→ "reason": "loose_items_not_enabled", 283→ "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis), 284→ "message": _("Enable 'Can be converted to BOM' for loose items first"), 285→ } 286→ 287→ if analysis["changed_components"]: 288→ # Categorize changed components by blocking level 289→ hard_blocked = [] # MR/RFQ blocks - user must deactivate old BOM 290→ manager_blocked = [] # PO blocks - need manager role 291→ confirmable = [] # No procurement - just need confirmation 292→ 293→ for comp in analysis["changed_components"]: 294→ details = comp.get("details", {}) 295→ blocking_level = details.get("blocking_level", "confirm") 296→ 297→ if blocking_level == "manager_required": 298→ if details.get("can_proceed"): 299→ # User has manager role, can confirm 300→ confirmable.append(comp) 301→ else: 302→ # User lacks manager role 303→ manager_blocked.append(comp) 304→ elif blocking_level == "block": 305→ hard_blocked.append(comp) 306→ else: 307→ # confirm level 308→ confirmable.append(comp) 309→ 310→ if hard_blocked: 311→ return { 312→ "status": "procurement_blocked", 313→ "reason": "active_mr_rfq", 314→ "blocked_components": [ 315→ { 316→ "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"], 317→ "details": c.get("details", {}), 318→ } 319→ for c in hard_blocked 320→ ], 321→ "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis), 322→ "message": _("Cannot change BOM. Deactivate old BOMs manually, then re-run upload."), 323→ } 324→ 325→ if manager_blocked: 326→ return { 327→ "status": "manager_required", 328→ "reason": "active_po_no_role", 329→ "blocked_components": [ 330→ { 331→ "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"], 332→ "details": c.get("details", {}), 333→ } 334→ for c in manager_blocked 335→ ], 336→ "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis), 337→ "message": _("Cannot change BOM. Confirm with remarks to proceed."), 352→ } 353→ 354→ # Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and link back to Component Masters 355→ result = create_boms_and_link_components(tree, doc.project, analysis, ws, image_loader, machine_code) 356→ 357→ # Build comprehensive summary 358→ result["summary"] = { 359→ "items": { 360→ "created": item_counters["created"], 361→ "existing": item_counters["existing"], 362→ "updated": item_counters["updated"], 363→ "failed": item_counters["failed"], 364→ "total": sum(item_counters.values()) 365→ }, 366→ "boms": { 367→ "created": result.pop("created", 0), 368→ "existing": result.pop("skipped", 0), 369→ "failed": result.pop("failed", 0), 370→ "total": 0 # Will be calculated below 371→ }, 372→ "component_masters": { 373→ "created": cm_counters["created"], 374→ "existing": cm_counters["existing"], 375→ "updated": cm_counters["updated"], 376→ "failed": cm_counters["failed"], 377→ "total": sum(cm_counters.values()) 378→ } 379→ } 380→ result["summary"]["boms"]["total"] = ( 381→ result["summary"]["boms"]["created"] + 382→ result["summary"]["boms"]["existing"] + 383→ result["summary"]["boms"]["failed"] 384→ ) 385→ 386→ # Ensure status is set explicitly 387→ result["status"] = "success" 388→ 389→ # Build user-friendly message 390→ result["message"] = _build_summary_message(result["summary"], result.get("errors", [])) 391→ 392→ # Log upload history 393→ doc.append("upload_history", { 394→ "bom_file": doc.bom_file, 395→ "machine_code": machine_code, 396→ "uploaded_by": frappe.session.user, 397→ "uploaded_on": frappe.utils.now_datetime(), 398→ "status": "Success", 399→ "items_created": result["summary"]["items"].get("created", 0), 400→ "boms_created": result["summary"]["boms"].get("created", 0), 401→ "cms_created": result["summary"]["component_masters"].get("created", 0), 402→ }) 403→ doc.save(ignore_permissions=True) 404→ 405→ frappe.log_error(title="BOM Upload Debug - Fresh Upload", message=str(result)) 406→ frappe.db.commit() 407→ return result 408→ 409→ 410→# ==================== Step 2: Ensure Items ==================== 411→ 412→def ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, counters=None): 413→ """ 414→ Walk tree and create Item masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items). 415→ Must run BEFORE Component Master creation since item_code is a Link field. 416→ Uses ensure_item_exists() from existing BOM Upload module (idempotent). 417→ 418→ Args: 419→ tree: List of root nodes 420→ ws: Excel worksheet 421→ image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None) 422→ counters: dict to track {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0} 423→ 424→ Returns: 425→ dict: counters 426→ """ 427→ if counters is None: 428→ counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0} 429→ 430→ for node in tree: 431→ result = ensure_item_exists( 432→ node["item_code"], 433→ node.get("description"), 434→ node.get("extended_description"), 435→ node.get("uom"), 436→ node.get("row_num"), 437→ ws, 438→ image_loader, 439→ node.get("material"), 440→ node.get("treatment"), 441→ node.get("weight"), 442→ node.get("part_number"), 443→ node.get("manufacturer"), 444→ node.get("revision") 445→ ) 446→ counters[result] = counters.get(result, 0) + 1 447→ 448→ if node.get("children"): 449→ ensure_items_for_all_nodes(node["children"], ws, image_loader, counters) 450→ 451→ return counters 452→ 453→ 454→# ==================== Step 3: Create Component Masters ==================== 455→ 456→def create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, project, machine_code): 457→ """ 458→ Create Component Masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf/RM items). 459→ For existing CMs: apply make/buy merge logic (Excel wins if present, else keep existing). 460→ For new CMs: set defaults based on item type. 461→ 462→ Business Rules: 463→ - All items default to "Buy" (conservative approach) 464→ - T, Y, E prefixed items: released_for_procurement = "No" 465→ - Other items: released_for_procurement = "Yes" 466→ - machine_code is set on all Component Masters (enables per-machine tracking) 467→ 468→ Args: 469→ tree: List of root nodes 470→ project: Project name 471→ machine_code: Machine code from BOM Upload (e.g., P0000000003033) 472→ 473→ Returns: 474→ dict: {"created": X, "existing": X, "updated": X, "failed": X} 475→ """ 476→ counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0} 477→ all_nodes = _get_all_nodes(tree) 478→ 479→ for node in all_nodes: 480→ item_code = node["item_code"] 481→ is_assembly = bool(node.get("children")) 482→ 483→ # Check if Component Master already exists (project + item_code + machine_code unique) 484→ existing = frappe.db.exists( 485→ "Project Component Master", 486→ { 487→ "project": project, 488→ "item_code": item_code, 489→ "machine_code": machine_code 490→ }, 491→ ) 492→ 493→ if existing: 494→ # Merge logic: update make_or_buy only if Excel has a value 495→ updated = _merge_make_or_buy(existing, node) 496→ if updated: 497→ counters["updated"] += 1 498→ else: 499→ counters["existing"] += 1 500→ continue Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Adds the new version as current (is_current=1, activated_on=now) 285→ 286→ Args: 287→ project: Project name 288→ item_code: Item code of the assembly 289→ old_bom_name: Name of the previous active BOM 290→ new_bom_name: Name of the new BOM being activated 291→ remarks: Optional remarks for the version change 292→ """ 293→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 294→ if not component_master: 295→ return 296→ 297→ now = frappe.utils.now_datetime() 298→ 299→ # Capture procurement snapshot for old BOM before deactivation 300→ procurement_snapshot = _capture_procurement_snapshot(project, old_bom_name) 301→ 302→ # Mark old version as deactivated and attach snapshot 303→ for row in component_master.bom_version_history: 304→ if row.bom_name == old_bom_name and row.is_current: 305→ row.deactivated_on = now 306→ row.is_current = 0 307→ row.procurement_snapshot = json.dumps(procurement_snapshot) 308→ break 309→ 310→ # Calculate new version number 311→ max_version = 0 312→ for row in component_master.bom_version_history: 313→ if row.version_number and row.version_number > max_version: 314→ max_version = row.version_number 315→ new_version_number = max_version + 1 316→ 317→ # Get structure hash for new BOM 318→ try: 319→ new_bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", new_bom_name) 320→ structure_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(new_bom_doc) 321→ except frappe.DoesNotExistError: 322→ structure_hash = None 323→ 324→ # Get BOM child items (consolidated) 383→ try: 384→ bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name) 385→ except frappe.DoesNotExistError: 386→ return snapshot 387→ 388→ # Consolidate duplicate items 389→ item_qty_map = {} 390→ for item in bom_doc.items: 391→ if item.item_code in item_qty_map: 392→ item_qty_map[item.item_code] += float(item.qty or 0) 393→ else: 394→ item_qty_map[item.item_code] = float(item.qty or 0) 395→ 396→ # For each child item, capture procurement data 397→ for item_code, qty_per_unit in item_qty_map.items(): 398→ child_snapshot = { 399→ "item_code": item_code, 400→ "qty_per_unit": qty_per_unit, 401→ "bom_qty_required": 0, 402→ "total_qty_limit": 0, 403→ "mr_refs": [], 404→ "mr_total_qty": 0, 405→ "rfq_refs": [], 406→ "rfq_total_qty": 0, 407→ "po_refs": [], 408→ "po_total_qty": 0, 409→ "pr_refs": [], 410→ "received_qty": 0 411→ } 412→ 413→ # Get Component Master data 414→ child_cm = get_component_master(project, item_code) 415→ if child_cm: 416→ child_snapshot["bom_qty_required"] = float(child_cm.bom_qty_required or 0) 417→ child_snapshot["total_qty_limit"] = float(child_cm.total_qty_limit or 0) 418→ 419→ # Get Material Requests 420→ mrs = frappe.db.sql(""" 421→ SELECT mr.name, mri.qty 422→ FROM `tabMaterial Request` mr 423→ INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request Item` mri ON mri.parent = mr.name 424→ WHERE mri.item_code = %(item_code)s 425→ AND mr.custom_project_ = %(project)s 426→ AND mr.docstatus != 2 427→ """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True) 428→ 429→ for mr in mrs: 430→ child_snapshot["mr_refs"].append(mr.name) 431→ child_snapshot["mr_total_qty"] += float(mr.qty or 0) 432→ 433→ # Get Purchase Orders 448→ pos = frappe.db.sql(""" 449→ SELECT po.name, poi.qty 450→ FROM `tabPurchase Order` po 451→ INNER JOIN `tabPurchase Order Item` poi ON poi.parent = po.name 452→ WHERE poi.item_code = %(item_code)s 453→ AND po.project = %(project)s 454→ AND po.docstatus != 2 455→ """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True) 456→ 457→ for po in pos: 458→ child_snapshot["po_refs"].append(po.name) 459→ child_snapshot["po_total_qty"] += float(po.qty or 0) 460→ 461→ # De-duplicate refs (in case same doc appears multiple times) 476→ child_snapshot["mr_refs"] = list(set(child_snapshot["mr_refs"])) 477→ child_snapshot["rfq_refs"] = list(set(child_snapshot["rfq_refs"])) 478→ child_snapshot["po_refs"] = list(set(child_snapshot["po_refs"])) 479→ child_snapshot["pr_refs"] = list(set(child_snapshot["pr_refs"])) 480→ 481→ snapshot["child_items"].append(child_snapshot) 482→ 483→ return snapshot 484→ 485→ 486→def _add_initial_bom_version(project, item_code, bom_name): 487→ """ 488→ Add the initial BOM version to history when a BOM is first linked. 489→ Called when active_bom was previously empty. 490→ 491→ Args: 492→ project: Project name 493→ item_code: Item code of the assembly 494→ bom_name: Name of the BOM being activated 495→ """ 496→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 497→ if not component_master: 498→ return 499→ 500→ # Add as version 1 515→ component_master.append("bom_version_history", { 516→ "bom_name": bom_name, 517→ "version_number": 1, 518→ "structure_hash": structure_hash, 519→ "activated_on": now, 520→ "is_current": 1, 521→ "change_remarks": "Initial BOM version" 522→ }) 523→ 524→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 525→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 526→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 527→ 528→ 529→def _backfill_bom_version_history(project, item_code, current_bom_name): 530→ """ 531→ Backfill version history for existing BOMs that were created before Component Master. 532→ 533→ This handles the case where: 534→ 1. Need to retroactively add old BOMs to version history 537→ 538→ Args: 539→ project: Project name 540→ item_code: Item code of the assembly 541→ current_bom_name: Name of the BOM being submitted now 542→ """ 543→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 544→ if not component_master: 545→ return 546→ 547→ # Get all submitted BOMs for this item+project (ordered by creation) 548→ all_boms = frappe.get_all( 549→ "BOM", 550→ filters={ 551→ "item": item_code, 552→ "project": project, 553→ "docstatus": 1 554→ }, 555→ fields=["name", "creation", "is_active", "is_default"], 556→ order_by="creation asc" 557→ ) 558→ 559→ if not all_boms: 560→ return 561→ 562→ # Filter out BOMs already in version history 563→ existing_bom_names = {row.bom_name for row in component_master.bom_version_history} 564→ boms_to_add = [b for b in all_boms if b.name not in existing_bom_names] 565→ 566→ if not boms_to_add: 567→ return 568→ 569→ # Determine if this is the current BOM 579→ is_current = 1 if bom.name == current_bom_name else 0 580→ deactivated_on = None if is_current else bom.creation # Use creation as deactivation placeholder 581→ 582→ component_master.append("bom_version_history", { 583→ "bom_name": bom.name, 584→ "version_number": version_num, 585→ "structure_hash": structure_hash, 586→ "activated_on": bom.creation, 587→ "deactivated_on": deactivated_on, 588→ "is_current": is_current, 589→ "change_remarks": f"Backfilled from existing BOM (created {bom.creation})" 590→ }) 591→ 592→ version_num += 1 593→ 594→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 595→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 596→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 597→ 598→ # new_items 673→ for removed_item in removed_items: 674→ mr_count = frappe.db.count( 675→ "Material Request Item", 676→ filters={ 677→ "item_code": removed_item, 678→ "parenttype": "Material Request", 679→ "docstatus": ["<", 2], 680→ } 681→ ) 682→ po_count = frappe.db.count( 683→ "Purchase Order Item", 684→ filters={ 685→ "item_code": removed_item, 686→ "parenttype": "Purchase Order", 687→ "docstatus": ["<", 2], 688→ } 689→ ) 690→ if mr_count or po_count: 691→ removed_items_alerts.append( 692→ f"{removed_item} ({mr_count} MR(s), {po_count} PO(s))" 693→ ) 694→ 695→ # Check 2: Items with QTY REDUCED (in both BOMs but new qty < old qty) 696→ common_items = old_items & new_items 697→ for common_item in common_items: 698→ old_qty = old_item_qty.get(common_item, 0) 699→ new_qty = new_item_qty.get(common_item, 0) 700→ 701→ if new_qty < old_qty: 702→ # Qty reduced - check if existing procurement exceeds new limit 703→ # Get total qty already procured (MR + PO) 704→ mr_qty_result = frappe.db.sql(""" 705→ SELECT COALESCE(SUM(mri.qty), 0) as total_qty 706→ FROM `tabMaterial Request` mr 707→ INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request Item` mri ON mri.parent = mr.name 708→ WHERE mri.item_code = %(item_code)s 709→ AND mr.custom_project_ = %(project)s 710→ AND mr.docstatus != 2 711→ """, {"item_code": common_item, "project": project}, as_dict=True) 712→ 713→ po_qty_result = frappe.db.sql(""" 714→ SELECT COALESCE(SUM(poi.qty), 0) as total_qty 715→ FROM `tabPurchase Order` po 716→ INNER JOIN `tabPurchase Order Item` poi ON poi.parent = po.name 717→ WHERE poi.item_code = %(item_code)s 718→ AND po.project = %(project)s 719→ AND po.docstatus != 2 720→ """, {"item_code": common_item, "project": project}, as_dict=True) 721→ 722→ total_procured = (mr_qty_result[0].total_qty if mr_qty_result else 0) + \ 723→ (po_qty_result[0].total_qty if po_qty_result else 0) 724→ 725→ # Get parent's project_qty to calculate new requirement 726→ parent_cm = get_component_master(project, item_code) 727→ parent_project_qty = float(parent_cm.project_qty or 1) if parent_cm else 1 728→ 729→ # New total requirement = new_qty * parent_project_qty 730→ new_requirement = new_qty * parent_project_qty 731→ old_requirement = old_qty * parent_project_qty 732→ 733→ if total_procured > new_requirement: 734→ over_qty = total_procured - new_requirement 735→ qty_reduced_alerts.append( 736→ f"{common_item}: BOM qty {old_qty} → {new_qty} " 737→ f"(requirement {old_requirement} → {new_requirement}). parent item and its Component Master. 950→ 951→ Hierarchy: Machine Code → M-Code → G-Code → D-Code/Raw Materials 952→ 953→ Rules: 954→ - If parent starts with "M" → m_code = parent_item, g_code = NULL 955→ - If parent starts with "G" → m_code = parent's m_code, g_code = parent_item 956→ - Otherwise → traverse parent_component chain to find nearest M and G 957→ 958→ Args: 959→ parent_cm: Parent Component Master (may be None) 960→ parent_item: Parent item code 961→ 962→ Returns: 963→ tuple: (m_code, g_code) 964→ """ 965→ if not parent_item: 966→ return None, None 967→ 968→ # If parent is M-code (root assembly) 969→ if parent_item.startswith("M"): 970→ return parent_item, None 971→ 972→ # If parent is G-code (sub-assembly) 973→ if parent_item.startswith("G"): 974→ # Get M-code from parent CM or traverse 975→ m_code = None 976→ if parent_cm: 977→ # Try to get m_code from parent CM 978→ m_code = parent_cm.m_code 979→ if not m_code: 980→ # Traverse up to find M-code 981→ m_code = _traverse_for_m_code(parent_cm) 982→ return m_code, parent_item 983→ 984→ # Parent is below G-level (D-code, raw material, etc.) 985→ # Need to traverse up to find both M-code and G-code 986→ if parent_cm: 987→ m_code = parent_cm.m_code or _traverse_for_m_code(parent_cm) 988→ g_code = parent_cm.g_code or _traverse_for_g_code(parent_cm) 989→ return m_code, g_code 990→ 991→ return None, None 992→ 993→ 994→def _traverse_for_m_code(cm): 995→ """ 996→ Traverse up the parent_component chain to find the M-code (item starting with M). 997→ """ 998→ if not cm: 999→ return None 1000→ 1001→ visited = set() 1002→ current = cm 1003→ 1004→ while current and current.name not in visited: 1005→ visited.add(current.name) 1006→ 1007→ # Check if current item is M-code 1008→ if current.item_code and current.item_code.startswith("M"): 1009→ return current.item_code 1010→ 1011→ # Check if current CM has m_code set 1012→ if current.m_code: 1013→ return current.m_code 1014→ 1015→ # Move to parent 1016→ if not current.parent_component: 1017→ break 1018→ try: 1019→ current = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", current.parent_component) 1020→ except frappe.DoesNotExistError: 1021→ break 1022→ 1023→ return None 1024→ 1025→ 1026→def _traverse_for_g_code(cm): 1027→ """ 1028→ Traverse up the parent_component chain to find the G-code (item starting with G). 1029→ """ 1030→ if not cm: 1031→ return None 1032→ 1033→ visited = set() 1034→ current = cm 1035→ 1036→ while current and current.name not in visited: 1037→ visited.add(current.name) 1038→ 1039→ # Check if current item is G-code 1040→ if current.item_code and current.item_code.startswith("G"): 1041→ return current.item_code 1042→ 1043→ # Check if current CM has g_code set 1044→ if current.g_code: 1045→ return current.g_code 1046→ 1047→ # Move to parent 1048→ if not current.parent_component: 1049→ break 1050→ try: 1051→ current = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", current.parent_component) 1052→ except frappe.DoesNotExistError: 1053→ break 1054→ 1055→ return None 1056→ 1057→ 1058→def remove_bom_usage(project, item_code, parent_bom): 1059→ """ 1060→ Remove a BOM Usage entry from the Component Master. 1061→ Silent skip if Component Master doesn't exist. 1062→ """ 1063→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 1064→ if not component_master: 1065→ return # Silent skip 1066→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 1079→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 1080→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 1081→ 1082→ frappe.msgprint( 1083→ _("Removed BOM Usage from Component Master {0}").format(component_master.name), 1084→ indicator="blue", 1085→ alert=True 1086→ ) 1087→ 1088→ 1089→def refresh_bom_usage(bom_doc): 1090→ """ 1091→ Refresh all BOM usage entries for a BOM. 1092→ Removes entries for items no longer in BOM, adds/updates current items. 1093→ """ 1094→ if not bom_doc.project: 1095→ return 1096→ 1097→ # Get current item codes in the Component Master", 1103→ filters={"project": bom_doc.project}, 1104→ fields=["name", "item_code"] 1105→ ) 1106→ 1107→ for cm in component_masters: 1108→ component_master = frappe.get_doc("Project BOM - update qty if changed 1119→ if row.qty_per_unit != current_items[cm.item_code]: 1120→ row.qty_per_unit = current_items[cm.item_code] 1121→ has_changes = True 1122→ else: 1123→ # Item no longer in BOM - mark for removal 1124→ rows_to_remove.append(idx) 1125→ has_changes = True 1126→ 1127→ # Remove obsolete rows 1128→ for idx in reversed(rows_to_remove): 1129→ component_master.remove(component_master.bom_usage[idx]) 1130→ 1131→ # Save if changes made 1132→ if has_changes: 1133→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 1134→ get_component_master(project, item_code) 1147→ if not component_master: 1148→ return # Silent skip 1149→ 1150→ # Get the BOM document 1151→ bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name) 1152→ 1153→ # component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 1180→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 1181→ 1182→ 1183→def calculate_bom_structure_hash(bom_doc): 1184→ """ 1185→ Calculate MD5 hash of BOM structure. 1186→ Based on sorted list of (item_code, qty) tuples. 1187→ """ 1188→ if not bom_doc.items: 1189→ return None 1190→ 1191→ # Create list of (item_code, qty) tuples, sorted by item_code 1192→ structure = sorted( 1193→ [(item.item_code, float(item.qty)) for item in bom_doc.items], 1194→ key=lambda x: x[0] 1195→ ) 1196→ 1197→ # Convert to JSON string and hash 1198→ structure_str = json.dumps(structure, sort_keys=True) 1199→ return hashlib.md5(structure_str.encode()).hexdigest() 1200→ 1201→ 1202→def recalculate_component_masters_for_project(project): 1203→ """ 1204→ Recalculate all Component Master quantities for a project. 1205→ 1206→ Call this AFTER frappe.db.commit() to ensure all BOM data is saved. 1207→ This runs synchronously to ensure values are immediately available. 1208→ 1209→ Args: 1210→ project: Project name 1211→ 1212→ Returns: 1213→ dict: Summary of recalculations (updated count, errors) 1214→ """ 1215→ component_masters = frappe.get_all( 1216→ "Project Component Master", 1217→ filters={"project": project}, 1218→ pluck="name" 1219→ ) 1220→ 1221→ updated = 0 1222→ errors = [] 1223→ 1224→ for cm_name in component_masters: 1225→ try: 1226→ cm = cm.calculate_procurement_totals() 1232→ cm.update_procurement_status() 1233→ 1234→ # Use frappe.db.set_value to persist calculated fields (bypasses save() in hooks) 1235→ "procurement_balance": cm.procurement_balance, 1240→ "procurement_status": cm.procurement_status 1241→ }, update_modified=False) 1242→ 1243→ updated += 1 1244→ 1245→ except Exception as e: 1246→ errors.append(f"{cm_name}: {str(e)}") 1247→ frappe.log_error(f"Error recalculating {cm_name}: {str(e)}", "Component Master Recalculation") 1248→ 1249→ # Final commit to persist all db_set operations "item_code": child_item, 1330→ }) 1331→ 1332→ # Block if any procurement exists 1333→ can_proceed = len(procurement_docs) == 0 1334→ 1335→ return { 1336→ "can_proceed": can_proceed, 1337→ rfq.status, 1360→ rfqi.qty 1361→ FROM `tabRequest for Quotation` rfq 1362→ INNER JOIN `tabRequest for Quotation Item` rfqi ON rfqi.parent = rfq.name 1363→ WHERE rfqi.item_code = %(item_code)s 1364→ AND rfq.custom_project = %(project)s 1365→ AND rfq.docstatus != 2 1366→ """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True) 1367→ 1368→ 1369→def _get_purchase_orders(project, item_code): `tabPurchase Order` po 1376→ INNER JOIN `tabPurchase Order Item` poi ON poi.parent = po.name 1377→ WHERE poi.item_code = %(item_code)s 1378→ AND po.project = %(project)s 1379→ AND po.docstatus != 2 1380→ """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True) 1381→ 1382→ 1383→# ==================== Utility Functions for Manual Fixes ==================== 1384→ 1385→@frappe.whitelist() Returns: 1397→ dict: Summary of changes made 1398→ """ 1399→ from clevertech.project_component_master.procurement_hooks import _get_bom_version_for_procurement 1400→ 1401→ cm = frappe.get_doc("Project bom_version", 1427→ "null_count": null_count, 1428→ "updated_count": updated_count, 1429→ "bom_version_used": _get_bom_version_for_procurement(cm, cm.project) if updated_count > 0 else None 1430→ } 1431→ 1432→ 1433→@frappe.whitelist() 1434→def fix_bom_version_history(component_master_name): 1435→ """ 1436→ Utility function to manually fix BOM version history for a Component Master. 1437→ 1438→ Use this to retroactively fix cases where: 1439→ - BOMs were created before Component Master existed 1440→ - Version history is incomplete or incorrect 1441→ 1442→ Args: 1443→ component_master_name: Name of the Component Master to fix 1444→ 1445→ Returns: 1446→ dict: Summary of changes made 1447→ """ 1448→ cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", component_master_name) 1449→ 1450→ if not cm.has_bom: 1451→ return { 1452→ "status": "skipped", 1453→ "message": "Component Master does not have BOMs" 1454→ } 1455→ 1456→ # Get all submitted BOMs for this item+project (ordered by creation) 1457→ all_boms = frappe.get_all( 1458→ "BOM", 1459→ filters={ 1460→ "item": cm.item_code, 1461→ "project": cm.project, 1462→ "docstatus": 1 1463→ }, 1464→ fields=["name", "creation", "is_active", "is_default"], 1465→ order_by="creation asc" 1466→ ) 1467→ 1468→ if not all_boms: 1469→ return { 1470→ "status": "skipped", 1471→ "message": "No submitted BOMs found for this item" 1472→ } 1473→ 1474→ # Clear existing version history 1475→ old_history = [(row.bom_name, row.version_number) for row in cm.bom_version_history] 1476→ cm.bom_version_history = [] 1477→ 1478→ # Rebuild version history from scratch 1479→ version_num = 1 1480→ for idx, bom in enumerate(all_boms): 1481→ try: 1482→ bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom.name) 1483→ structure_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(bom_doc) 1484→ except frappe.DoesNotExistError: 1485→ structure_hash = None 1486→ 1487→ # Last BOM in the list is the most recent (current) 1488→ is_last = (idx == len(all_boms) - 1) 1489→ is_current = 1 if is_last else 0 1490→ 1491→ # Deactivation date: use next BOM's creation date, or None if current 1492→ if is_last: 1493→ deactivated_on = None 1494→ else: 1495→ deactivated_on = all_boms[idx + 1].creation 1496→ 1497→ cm.append("bom_version_history", { 1498→ "bom_name": bom.name, 1499→ "version_number": version_num, 1500→ "structure_hash": structure_hash, 1501→ "activated_on": bom.creation, 1502→ "deactivated_on": deactivated_on, 1503→ "is_current": is_current, 1504→ "change_remarks": f"Rebuilt version history (originally created {bom.creation.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')})" 1505→ }) 1506→ 1507→ version_num += 1 1508→ 1509→ # Update active_bom to the most recent BOM 1510→ if all_boms: 1511→ cm.active_bom = all_boms[-1].name 1512→ 1513→ cm.flags.ignore_validate = True 1514→ cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 1515→ cm.save(ignore_permissions=True) 1516→ 1517→ frappe.db.commit() 1518→ 1519→ return { 1520→ "status": "success", 1521→ "message": f"Rebuilt version history with {len(all_boms)} BOM(s)", 1522→ "old_history": old_history, 1523→ "new_history": [(row.bom_name, row.version_number) for row in cm.bom_version_history], 1524→ "active_bom": cm.active_bom 1525→ } 1526→
